# sshbbs

A simplified PTT-style BBS served over SSH. Built with
[charmbracelet/wish](https://github.com/charmbracelet/wish) +
[bubbletea](https://github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea), backed by SQLite
(pure-Go via `modernc.org/sqlite`).

![demo](docs/demo.gif)

> Vim-style `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` navigation alongside arrow keys & Esc/Enter.
> The recording above shows login → boards → article → 推/噓 → `Ctrl+U`
> water-balloon inbox → disconnect, all driven from the keyboard.
> Re-record with `./scripts/record-demo.sh`.

Implements the MUST-HAVE feature set of the planning doc:

- Account register + login over SSH (bcrypt-hashed passwords)
- Default boards: `Welcome`, `Test`, `ChitChat`
- Read & post articles (UTF-8, CJK-aware widths via `go-runewidth`)
- 推文 (推 / 噓 / →) with live broadcast to other viewers of the same article
- 水球 (water balloons) — private one-line messages with offline persistence
  and on-reconnect replay

## Quickstart

```bash
make hostkey            # generates .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once)
make run                # starts the server on :2222

# In another terminal, register:
ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password is ignored — fill the in-TUI form

# Then log in:
ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password you registered with
```

## Local dev with Docker

If you'd rather not install Go locally:

```bash
make compose-up         # builds, generates a host key into a named volume, runs on :2222
ssh new@localhost -p 2222
make compose-down
```

The `bbs-data` and `bbs-keys` volumes persist across restarts so accounts
and the host key survive `compose-down`/`compose-up`. See
[`docs/operations/04_docker.md`](docs/operations/04_docker.md) for what the
multi-stage build and `hostkey-init` service do, and a warning about
volume drivers that don't honour SQLite's filesystem locks (don't put
`bbs-data` on NFS).

Inside the TUI:

- `↑`/`↓` or `j`/`k` to move, `Enter` to select
- `p` in a board view to post
- `+` / `-` / `=` in an article view for 推 / 噓 / →
- `Ctrl+U` from anywhere logged-in to open the water-balloon inbox; `c` to
  compose, `r` to reply
- `Esc` / `Backspace` to navigate back
- `Ctrl+C` to disconnect

## Two-user demo

```bash
# T1
ssh alice@localhost -p 2222

# T2
ssh bob@localhost -p 2222

# From alice: Ctrl+U → c → bob → "hi" → Ctrl+S
# Bob sees a toast in the footer within ~100ms.
```

## Common gotchas

- **`Host key verification failed`** after running `make hostkey` again:

  ```bash
  ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'
  ```

- **CJK looks like garbage**: the client terminal must be UTF-8.

  ```bash
  echo $LANG     # should match *.UTF-8
  ```

- **`SQLITE_BUSY`** under high write load: the pure-Go driver is slower than
  the CGO `mattn/go-sqlite3`. WAL + a 5s busy_timeout + a process-level
  `writeMu` keeps us comfortable for dozens of users; swap drivers if it bites.

## Layout

```
cmd/sshbbs/             entrypoint, flag parsing, signal handling
internal/server/        wish bootstrap + password-auth middleware
internal/auth/          register/login (bcrypt, validation)
internal/store/         SQLite handle, migrations, repos
internal/store/migrations/  *.sql, embedded with go:embed
internal/chat/          in-memory broker (presence + live send)
internal/tui/           bubbletea root + per-screen models
docs/ptt_trace_code/    notes mapping pttbbs concepts to our schema
```

## Development

```bash
make build              # build the binary
make watch              # auto-rebuild on .go/.sql changes (dev loop)
make test               # run tests
make test-race          # race detector (CI standard)
make cover              # coverage by package
make db-reset           # delete the SQLite DB
make tidy               # go mod tidy
make fmt vet            # gofmt + go vet
```

`make watch` uses [`air`](https://github.com/air-verse/air), pinned via the
Go 1.24+ `tool` directive (`go tool air`) — no global install needed.
Edits to `.go` or `.sql` files trigger SIGINT → graceful drain → rebuild →
relaunch; active SSH clients get disconnected and just reconnect.

See [`docs/development.md`](docs/development.md) for the full local-dev
playbook (watcher tuning, Delve step-debugger, test-watch recipes,
graceful-shutdown contract) and [`docs/testing.md`](docs/testing.md) for
the layered testing strategy.

## Roadmap & lessons learned

> See `docs/ptt_trace_code/00_overview.md` for what we deliberately don't
> implement, and the project plan
> `./.claude/plans/ptt-bbs-user-register-account-gentle-cookie.md` for the P1 / P2 backlog.

Forward-looking work — long-term ideas, deferred items, things needing
evaluation — lives in [`TODO.md`](TODO.md), prioritised P1 → P3 with effort
estimates (S/M/L/XL). Items with accompanying research, design notes, or paused
troubleshooting link to a corresponding [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/) doc.

Backward-looking knowledge — past traps and non-obvious debugging — lives in
[`pitfalls/`](pitfalls/), titled by symptom so future-you can grep the error
message and land on the root cause + workaround instead of re-debugging from
scratch.

## Resources

- [ptt/pttbbs: PTT BBS source code](https://github.com/ptt/pttbbs)
  - [ptt/pttbbs | DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/ptt/pttbbs)
  - [Home · ptt/pttbbs Wiki](https://github.com/ptt/pttbbs/wiki)
- [批踢踢 - 維基百科，自由的百科全書](https://zh.wikipedia.org/zh-tw/%E6%89%B9%E8%B8%A2%E8%B8%A2)
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What this is

A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
they remain usable as text-edit keys.

For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.

## Common commands

```bash
make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
make watch              # auto-rebuild on .go/.sql changes; SIGINT-then-restart via `air`
make build              # produces ./sshbbs
make test               # plain go test
make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
make cover              # per-package coverage
make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*

# focused testing
go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
go test -race ./internal/chat/...

# manual two-user smoke
ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
```

If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.

## Architecture — the non-obvious parts

### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"

8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

12	)
13	
14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
15	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
16	type Deps struct {
17		Store              *store.Store
18		Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
19		User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
20		IsRegister         bool
21		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
25	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
26	// and the toast footer.
27	type Root struct {
28		deps   Deps
29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	}
36	
37	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
38	
39	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
40	//
41	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
42	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
43	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
44	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
45		r := Root{deps: deps}
46		switch {
47		case deps.IsRegister:
48			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
49		case deps.MustChangePassword:
50			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
51		default:
52			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
53		}
54		return r
55	}
56	
57	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
58		return m.sub.Init()
59	}
60	
61	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
62		switch msg := msg.(type) {
63		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
64			m.width = msg.Width
65			m.height = msg.Height
66			var cmd tea.Cmd
67			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
68			return m, cmd
69	
70		case tea.KeyMsg:
71			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
72			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			}
75			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
76			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
77				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
78			}
79	
80		case NavigateMsg:
81			return m.navigate(msg)
82	
83		case ErrorMsg:
84			if msg.Err != nil {
85				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
86			}
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case WBIncomingMsg:
90			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
91			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
92			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
93				go func(id int64) {
94					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
95				}(msg.ID)
96			}
97			// fall through so the active screen can also react
98		}
99	
100		var cmd tea.Cmd
101		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
102		return m, cmd
103	}
104	
105	func (m Root) View() string {
106		v := m.sub.View()
107		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
108			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
109		}
110		return v
111	}
112	
113	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
114		m.toast = s
115		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
116	}
117	
118	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
119	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
120	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
121		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
122			return nil
123		}
124		switch to {
125		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
126			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
132	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
133	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
134		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
135			return nil
136		}
137		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
138			return nil
139		}
140		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
141	}
142	
143	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
144		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
145			return m, cmd
146		}
147		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
148			return m, cmd
149		}
150		var sub tea.Model

28	
29	func scanMail(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Mail, error) {
30		m := &Mail{}
31		if err := row.Scan(
32			&m.ID, &m.ThreadID, &m.ParentID, &m.FromUserID, &m.FromUserIDStr,
33			&m.ToUserID, &m.Subject, &m.Body, &m.ReadAt, &m.CreatedAt,
34		); err != nil {

36		}

40	// Insert creates a new mail. If parentID is nil, this is a thread root and
41	// thread_id is back-filled to the new row's own id under writeMu so the
42	// invariant `thread_id IS NOT NULL` holds for every read.
43	func (r *MailRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, subject, body string, parentID *int64) (*Mail, error) {
44		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
45		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
46	
47		var threadID sql.NullInt64
48		var parent sql.NullInt64
49		if parentID != nil {
50			parent = sql.NullInt64{Int64: *parentID, Valid: true}
51			// Look up the parent's thread_id so the new reply joins the same thread.
52			row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT thread_id FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, *parentID)

54			if err := row.Scan(&t); err != nil {
55				if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
56					return nil, ErrMailNotFound
57				}
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			threadID = t
61		}
62	
63		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
64			`INSERT INTO mail (thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
65			threadID, parent, fromID, fromUserID, toID, subject, body,
66		)
67		if err != nil {
68			return nil, err
69		}
70		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
71		if err != nil {
72			return nil, err
73		}
74		if !threadID.Valid {
75			// Root mail — thread_id = id.
76			if _, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE mail SET thread_id = ? WHERE id = ?`, id, id); err != nil {
77				return nil, err
78			}
79		}
80		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, id)
81		return scanMail(row)
82	}

### 1. UI / Compose Path
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb.go`

The water balloon compose screen captures two pieces of information:
- **Recipient** (`to` field, line 147-151): Text input for the target userid, max 12 characters
- **Message body** (`body` field, line 153-157): Textarea input, max 240 characters

The compose flow:
1. User enters recipient userid (Tab/Enter to move to body)
2. User enters message body
3. Ctrl+S to submit (line 189)
4. `submit()` method (line 221) validates both fields, looks up the recipient in the users table, then calls `Insert()`

---

### 2. Broker / Send Path
**Files:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb.go:249-264` and `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/chat/broker.go:71-79`

The send path:
1. **Persist first** (line 249): Water balloon is inserted into the database via `m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, target.ID, body, false)` — the last `false` indicates it wasn't delivered live.
2. **Then attempt live send** (line 254-259): Uses **`broker.Send(target.ID, msg)` — single recipient delivery**. The broker is passed a `WBIncomingMsg{ID, FromUserID, Body}` struct.
3. **Conditional MarkRead** (line 260-262): If the broker returned `true` (recipient is online), the water balloon is immediately marked as read in the DB since it was delivered live.

The Broker's `Send()` method (broker.go:71-79) delivers to **every live session** of the recipient (supporting multiple SSH connections per user):
```go
func (b *Broker) Send(toUID int64, msg tea.Msg) bool {
    list := b.sessions[toUID]
    for _, s := range list {
        s.Program.Send(msg)
    }
    return len(list) > 0  // true if recipient is online
}
```

This is **NOT a broadcast** — it targets a specific user ID. There is no `SendToAll` usage for water balloons.

---

### 3. Receive / Display Path
**Files:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/root.go:89-97` and `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/server/server.go` (unread replay section)

**Live delivery (recipient online):**
- The `WBIncomingMsg` arrives at the Root model's Update() 
- Root immediately renders a **toast notification** in the footer: `"💧 " + FromUserID + ": " + Body` (line 90)
- Toast displays for 3 seconds (line 37: `const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second`)
- Simultaneously, Root marks the message as read in the DB (line 92-95)

**Offline delivery (recipient offline):**
- Water balloon is persisted with `read_at = NULL`
- On the next reconnect, the server drains unread water balloons from the DB and replays them as toasts (server.go, unread drain section)
- Each unread WB is replayed by sending a `WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body}` to the program

**No dedicated "inbox" interrupt overlay** — water balloons are always toasts. However, there IS a dedicated water balloon **inbox screen** (ScreenWBInbox, screen_wb.go:20-124) where the user can view their history by pressing Ctrl+U, listing all water balloons (read and unread) sorted unread-first.

---

### 4. Persistence — The Key Question
**Critical Finding: Water balloons CURRENTLY HAVE FULL PERSISTENCE**

The entire `water_balloons` table is defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql:57-68`:

```sql
CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,
    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    read_at         DATETIME,
    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
```

**Survival scenarios:**

a. **Receiver offline when sent** ✓ SURVIVES
   - Water balloon is inserted with `delivered_live = 0` and `read_at = NULL`
   - Persists in DB indefinitely

b. **Receiver disconnects and reconnects** ✓ SURVIVES
   - On reconnect, `ListUnreadFor()` (waterballoons.go:58-76) queries all water balloons where `read_at IS NULL`
   - Each unread WB is replayed as a toast via `WBIncomingMsg`
   - After viewing (or when the inbox is opened), `MarkRead()` sets `read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`

c. **Server restart** ✓ SURVIVES
   - All water balloons are persisted in SQLite
   - On the next user login, the same unread-drain mechanism replays them
   - No in-memory ring buffer or history in the broker — all replay comes from the DB

**Important distinction:** The `delivered_live` flag (line 63) tracks whether a water balloon was delivered while the recipient was online, but does **NOT** affect persistence. Both live and offline deliveries survive indefinitely until marked read.

---

### 5. Existing Chat Infrastructure
**File:** `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/chat/broker.go`

The Broker is **purely a pub/sub fan-out mechanism for live events**. It is NOT a chat-room concept:
- `Register(session)` — adds a user's active SSH connection to an in-memory map
- `Send(userID, msg)` — delivers a message to all live sessions of that user
- `SendToAll(excludeUID, msg)` — broadcasts to everyone online except the sender
- `OnlineList()` — returns list of currently logged-in users

**Usage across the codebase:**
- Water balloons: `Send(toUID, ...)` for 1-to-1 delivery
- Push notifications (on articles): `SendToAll(excludeUID, ...)` for broadcast to article viewers
- Mail notifications: similar pattern
- Article/board updates: `SendToAll(...)` for live refresh

**There is NO multi-user chat room or lobby screen.** All message types are either:
- 1-to-1 (water balloons, mail)
- Article-scoped (pushes)
- Global broadcast (announcements)

Screens available (from `screen_*.go` listing):
- `screen_admin_users` — user management
- `screen_article_view` — read/push on articles
- `screen_article_edit` — edit article
- `screen_board_view` — list articles
- `screen_main_menu` — nav hub
- `screen_mail` — mailbox (persistent 1-to-1 threads)
- `screen_online` — see who's online, quick-send water balloon
- `screen_post_compose` — post article
- `screen_wb` — water balloon inbox & compose
- `screen_password_change`, `screen_register`, etc.

No "chat room" or "lobby" screen exists.

---

### 6. Database Schema Overview
All tables from migrations:

**0001_init.sql:**
- `users` — accounts (bcrypt password, roles, login tracking)
- `boards` — article categories
- `articles` — posts (with recommend_score, filemode)
- `pushes` — reactions to articles (推/噓/→)
- `water_balloons` — 1-to-1 instant messages (transient UI, persistent DB)

**0003_add_mail.sql:**
- `mail` — persistent threaded private messages (unlike water balloons, these are intentionally durable and appear as a separate "mailbox" feature with subject + threading)

**0004_add_user_roles.sql:**
- Adds `role` and `must_change_password` columns to `users`

**0005_articles_updated_at.sql:**
- Adds `updated_at` tracking to `articles`

**0006_boards_banner.sql:**
- Adds `banner` column to `boards` (per-board ASCII art)

**0007_articles_pinned_at.sql:**
- Adds `pinned_at` column to `articles` (pin articles as 板規/announcements)

---

## Planning Implications

**Current state:**
- Water balloons already persist end-to-end (they're not transient despite the toast UI)
- They survive offline periods, disconnects, and server restarts
- They are stored in a dedicated `water_balloons` table with read/unread tracking
- No history is kept in the broker — all replay is DB-sourced

**Option A (add persistence): Already done!**
- The infrastructure is already there — you can immediately start showing history beyond just the unread set
- Modify the inbox screen to show read + unread water balloons (it already does via `ListInboxFor()` which shows both)
- Current behavior: inbox shows newest-first, with unread bubbled to the top

**Option B (build a full chat feature):**
- You'd want to decide: is "chat" replacing water balloons or living alongside them?
  - **Alongside:** Water balloons remain quick 1-liners with toast notifications; chat is a persistent multi-message thread view (similar to mail, but for any user pair)
  - **Replacement:** Unify under a single messages table with optional threading
- The `mail` table already implements the threading pattern you'd want (`thread_id`, `parent_id`)
- You'd need a new screen (e.g., `screen_chat` or extend the mail UI)
- The broker's `Send()` mechanism already works for user-to-user delivery
- Consider: should water balloons auto-populate as the first message in a chat thread when you click "reply"?

---

**File references for implementation:**
- UI compose: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb.go`
- Persistence repo: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/waterballoons.go`
- Broker: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/chat/broker.go`
- Message types: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/messages.go`
- Root receiver: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/root.go:89-97`
- Server reconnect replay: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/server/server.go` (search "Drain unread")
- Schema: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql:57-68` (water_balloons) and `0003_add_mail.sql` (mail structure reference)

29	func newWBInboxModel(deps Deps) wbInboxModel {

41	func (m wbInboxModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
42		switch msg := msg.(type) {
43		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
44			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
45			return m, nil
46		case tea.KeyMsg:
47			switch msg.String() {
48			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
49				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
50			case "up", "k":
51				if m.cursor > 0 {
52					m.cursor--
53				}
54			case "down", "j":
55				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
56					m.cursor++
57				}
58			case "c":
59				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose} }
60			case "r", "enter", " ", "right", "l":
61				if len(m.items) == 0 {
62					return m, nil
63				}
64				it := m.items[m.cursor]
65				return m, func() tea.Msg {
66					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: it.FromUserIDStr}
67				}
68			}
69		}
70		return m, nil
71	}
72	
73	func (m wbInboxModel) View() string {
74		var b strings.Builder
75		b.WriteString("\n")
76		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 水球 Water Balloons "))
77		b.WriteString("\n\n")
78	
79		if m.loadErr != nil {
80			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
81			return b.String()
82		}
83		if len(m.items) == 0 {
84			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no messages — press c to compose)") + "\n")
85			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("c compose · Esc/←/h back"))
86			return b.String()
87		}
88	
89		const (
90			dateW   = 16
91			fromW   = 14
92			statusW = 5
93		)
94		bodyW := max(20, m.width-2-dateW-1-fromW-1-statusW-3)
95		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",

98			PadRight("•", statusW),

101		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")

113			)

116			} else {

138		body   textarea.Model

146	func newWBComposeModel(deps Deps, recipient string) wbComposeModel {
147		to := textinput.New()
148		to.Placeholder = "alice"
149		to.CharLimit = 12
150		to.Width = 30
151		to.SetValue(recipient)
152	
153		body := textarea.New()
154		body.Placeholder = "your one-line message…"
155		body.CharLimit = 240
156		body.SetWidth(60)
157		body.SetHeight(4)
158	
159		m := wbComposeModel{deps: deps, to: to, body: body}
160		if recipient == "" {
161			m.focus = wbFocusTo
162			m.to.Focus()
163		} else {
164			m.focus = wbFocusBody
165			m.body.Focus()
166		}
167		return m
168	}
169	
170	func (m wbComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
171	
172	func (m wbComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
173		switch msg := msg.(type) {
174		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
175			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
176			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-12))
177			return m, nil
178	
179		case tea.KeyMsg:
180			if m.sent != "" {
181				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
182			}
183			switch msg.String() {
184			case "esc":
185				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
186			case "tab":
187				m.toggleFocus()
188				return m, nil
189			case "ctrl+s":
190				return m.submit()
191			}
192	
193			if m.focus == wbFocusTo {
194				if k := msg.String(); k == "enter" {
195					m.toggleFocus()
196					return m, nil
197				}
198				var cmd tea.Cmd
199				m.to, cmd = m.to.Update(msg)
200				return m, cmd
201			}
202			var cmd tea.Cmd
203			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
204			return m, cmd
205		}
206		return m, nil
207	}
208	
209	func (m *wbComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
210		if m.focus == wbFocusTo {
211			m.focus = wbFocusBody
212			m.to.Blur()
213			m.body.Focus()
214		} else {
215			m.focus = wbFocusTo
216			m.body.Blur()
217			m.to.Focus()
218		}
219	}
220	
221	func (m wbComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
222		to := strings.TrimSpace(m.to.Value())
223		body := strings.TrimSpace(m.body.Value())
224		if to == "" {
225			m.err = "recipient required"
226			return m, nil
227		}
228		if body == "" {
229			m.err = "body required"
230			return m, nil
231		}
232	
233		ctx := context.Background()
234		target, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, to)
235		if err != nil {
236			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
237				m.err = "no such user: " + to
238			} else {
239				m.err = err.Error()
240			}
241			return m, nil
242		}
243		from := m.deps.User
244	
245		delivered := false
246		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
247			// Persist first, then notify with the canonical row's ID.
248		}
249		wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, target.ID, body, false)
250		if err != nil {
251			m.err = err.Error()
252			return m, nil
253		}
254		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
255			delivered = m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, WBIncomingMsg{
256				ID:         wb.ID,
257				FromUserID: from.UserID,
258				Body:       body,
259			})
260			if delivered {
261				// The recipient saw it live, so we can mark it read immediately.
262				_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID)
263			}
264		}
265		m.sent = target.UserID
266		return m, nil
267	}
268	
269	func (m wbComposeModel) View() string {
270		var b strings.Builder
271		b.WriteString("\n")
272		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 丟水球 Send Water Balloon "))
273		b.WriteString("\n\n")
274	
275		if m.sent != "" {
276			b.WriteString("  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ sent to "+m.sent) + "\n\n")
277			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("any key returns to inbox"))
278			return b.String()
279		}
280	
281		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("To (userid):") + "\n")
282		b.WriteString("  " + m.to.View() + "\n\n")
283		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
284		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
285	
286		if m.err != "" {
287			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
288		}
289		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in To) → body · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
290		return b.String()
291	}

 `./internal/store/waterballoons.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	type WaterBalloon struct {
10		ID            int64
11		FromUserID    int64
12		FromUserIDStr string
13		ToUserID      int64
14		Body          string
15		DeliveredLive bool
16		ReadAt        sql.NullTime
17		CreatedAt     time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type WaterBalloonRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	const wbColumns = `id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live, read_at, created_at`
23	
24	func scanWB(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
25		w := &WaterBalloon{}
26		var deliveredLive int64
27		if err := row.Scan(&w.ID, &w.FromUserID, &w.FromUserIDStr, &w.ToUserID, &w.Body, &deliveredLive, &w.ReadAt, &w.CreatedAt); err != nil {
28			return nil, err
29		}
30		w.DeliveredLive = deliveredLive != 0
31		return w, nil
32	}
33	
34	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, body string, deliveredLive bool) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
35		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
36		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
37		live := int64(0)
38		if deliveredLive {
39			live = 1
40		}
41		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
42			`INSERT INTO water_balloons (from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
43			fromID, fromUserID, toID, body, live,
44		)
45		if err != nil {
46			return nil, err
47		}
48		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
49		if err != nil {
50			return nil, err
51		}
52		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE id = ?`, id)
53		return scanWB(row)
54	}
55	
56	// ListUnreadFor returns unread water balloons for the recipient, oldest
57	// first, so the TUI can replay them on reconnect.
58	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListUnreadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
59		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
60			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL ORDER BY id ASC`,
61			toUserID,
62		)
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66		defer rows.Close()
67		var out []*WaterBalloon
68		for rows.Next() {
69			w, err := scanWB(rows)
70			if err != nil {
71				return nil, err
72			}
73			out = append(out, w)
74		}
75		return out, rows.Err()
76	}
77	
78	// ListInboxFor returns the recipient's inbox: unread first (newest unread
79	// up top), then read messages newest-first.
80	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListInboxFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64, limit int) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
81		if limit <= 0 {
82			limit = 50
83		}
84		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
85			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE to_user_id = ?
86			 ORDER BY (read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
87			toUserID, limit,
88		)
89		if err != nil {
90			return nil, err
91		}
92		defer rows.Close()
93		var out []*WaterBalloon
94		for rows.Next() {
95			w, err := scanWB(rows)
96			if err != nil {
97				return nil, err
98			}
99			out = append(out, w)
100		}
101		return out, rows.Err()
102	}
103	
104	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkRead(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
105		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
106		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
107		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
108			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, id,
109		)
110		return err
111	}
112	
113	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkAllReadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) error {
114		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
115		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
116		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
117			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`,
118			toUserID,
119		)
120		return err
121	}
122
```

```text
Self-target is not blocked. I have everything needed. Now I'll deliver the plan as my final assistant message.

---

# Phase 1 Implementation Plan: 水球 Conversation Threads

A conversation-thread layer over the existing `water_balloons` table — no schema migration. Inbox switches from a flat list to a per-counterparty roll-up; Enter drills into a chronological two-direction transcript with that user; read-marking moves from "open inbox" to "open this thread."

## 1. Repo additions: `internal/store/waterballoons.go`

Three new methods. No new index needed for Phase 1 (BBS-scale data: per-recipient row counts are small, and the existing `idx_wb_to_unread (to_user_id, read_at)` already covers `MarkConversationRead`'s `WHERE`. The two-way `ListConversation` query plans as two index seeks via SQLite's OR-to-UNION rewrite — fine until per-pair history grows to the thousands; revisit if we ever build "load older").

### 1a. Counterparty struct (new)

```go
// WBCounterparty is one row of the per-counterparty inbox roll-up. ID/UserID
// are the OTHER user (not the viewer).
type WBCounterparty struct {
    UserID      int64
    UserIDStr   string
    LastBody    string
    LastFromMe  bool
    LastAt      time.Time
    UnreadCount int64 // unread *to* the viewer from this counterparty
}
```

`LastBody` / `LastFromMe` are nice-to-have for the inbox preview row ("you: …" vs "alice: …"). Add now since it costs one more column in the SELECT and matches Mail-inbox conventions.

### 1b. `ListCounterpartiesFor(ctx, userID, limit) ([]*WBCounterparty, error)`

The trickiest piece. The "derived counterparty id" (`CASE WHEN from_user_id = ? THEN to_user_id ELSE from_user_id END`) is computed once in a CTE so we can `GROUP BY` it cheaply. Then we LEFT JOIN that aggregate against the latest message row per counterparty to get `LastBody` / `LastFromMe`.

```sql
WITH related AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        from_user_id,
        from_userid,
        to_user_id,
        body,
        read_at,
        created_at,
        CASE WHEN from_user_id = ?1 THEN to_user_id ELSE from_user_id END AS cp_id
    FROM water_balloons
    WHERE from_user_id = ?1 OR to_user_id = ?1
),
agg AS (
    SELECT
        cp_id,
        MAX(id)   AS last_id,
        MAX(created_at) AS last_at,
        SUM(CASE WHEN to_user_id = ?1 AND read_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unread_count
    FROM related
    GROUP BY cp_id
)
SELECT
    a.cp_id,
    u.user_id     AS cp_userid_str,
    r.body        AS last_body,
    (r.from_user_id = ?1) AS last_from_me,
    a.last_at,
    a.unread_count
FROM agg a
JOIN related r ON r.id = a.last_id
JOIN users    u ON u.id = a.cp_id
ORDER BY (a.unread_count > 0) DESC, a.last_at DESC
LIMIT ?2;
```

Notes:
- `MAX(id)` not `MAX(created_at)` for last-row resolution; `id` is monotonic, `created_at` resolves to seconds in SQLite and can tie. `last_at` for display is still `MAX(created_at)` so the column reads naturally.
- The outer `ORDER BY (unread_count > 0) DESC, last_at DESC` puts conversations with unread first (matches `ListInboxFor`'s "unread first" convention from `waterballoons.go:86`), tie-broken by recency.
- Joining `users` makes the result tolerant of a renamed counterparty: we always show their *current* `user_id` (handle), not whatever was denormalized into `from_userid` on insert. See concern #3 below.
- `?1` and `?2` use sqlite numbered params — SQLite accepts both `?` and `?N`; modernc/sqlite supports it. If you want strict positional, repeat `?` and pass `userID` three times then `limit`.

### 1c. `ListConversation(ctx, viewerID, counterpartyID, limit) ([]*WaterBalloon, error)`

```sql
SELECT id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live, read_at, created_at
FROM water_balloons
WHERE (from_user_id = ? AND to_user_id = ?)
   OR (from_user_id = ? AND to_user_id = ?)
ORDER BY id ASC
LIMIT ?
```

Args: `(viewerID, counterpartyID, counterpartyID, viewerID, limit)`. ASC order so the newest is at the bottom (chat-style scrollback). `LIMIT 200` default. Phase 1 doesn't paginate; if a conversation exceeds the limit we silently truncate the oldest. That's acceptable for now — note in a TODO comment.

### 1d. `MarkConversationRead(ctx, viewerID, counterpartyID) error`

```sql
UPDATE water_balloons
SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE to_user_id = ?
  AND from_user_id = ?
  AND read_at IS NULL
```

Holds `s.writeMu` like the other mutators (`waterballoons.go:104`). Idempotent by design — second call no-ops because `read_at IS NULL` filter is empty.

## 2. Inbox screen rework: `internal/tui/screen_wb.go`

Change `wbInboxModel` from a flat-message list to a counterparty list.

### 2a. Constructor changes (`screen_wb.go:29-37`)

- Replace items field type: `items []*store.WaterBalloon` → `items []*store.WBCounterparty`.
- Call `ListCounterpartiesFor` instead of `ListInboxFor`.
- **Delete the `MarkAllReadFor` call at line 34** (the entire `if err == nil {…}` block). Read-marking now happens in the thread screen.

### 2b. Update changes (`screen_wb.go:41-71`)

- `r`/`enter`/`space`/`right`/`l` now navigates to `ScreenWBThread` (not `ScreenWBCompose`), passing the counterparty:
  ```go
  it := m.items[m.cursor]
  return m, func() tea.Msg {
      return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: it.UserID}
  }
  ```
- `c` continues to open `ScreenWBCompose` with no recipient (compose-anyone path).
- Optional but cheap: add `Ctrl+R` "refresh" so a user who lingers on the inbox can pull new counterparty rows after a `WBIncomingMsg`. Defer auto-refresh on `WBIncomingMsg` to a separate Update case (see §4).

### 2c. View changes (`screen_wb.go:73-124`)

Columns: Date · From · Unread · Last message preview ("you: hi" / "alice: hi"). Keep `PadRight`/`Truncate` and the existing column-width math (`screen_wb.go:89-100`). Replace `it.FromUserIDStr` → `it.UserIDStr`, `it.Body` → preview, `status := "NEW" / "·"` → `unreadCount` printed when > 0:

```go
status := "·"
if it.UnreadCount > 0 {
    status = fmt.Sprintf("%d", it.UnreadCount)
}
preview := it.LastBody
if it.LastFromMe {
    preview = "you: " + preview
}
```

Help line: `↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · c compose · Esc/←/h back`.

## 3. New thread screen: `wbThreadModel`

Keep the file lean — add to the bottom of `screen_wb.go` (mirrors how compose lives there). The inbox + compose + thread are tightly coupled and ~200 lines combined; one file is more readable than three.

### 3a. Constructor

```go
type wbThreadModel struct {
    deps     Deps
    cpID     int64           // counterparty user.ID
    cpUserID string          // counterparty handle (for header + reply path)
    items    []*store.WaterBalloon
    scroll   int             // top-line offset; clamped in View
    width    int
    height   int
    loadErr  error
}

func newWBThreadModel(deps Deps, counterpartyID int64) wbThreadModel {
    ctx := context.Background()
    cp, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, counterpartyID)
    if err != nil {
        return wbThreadModel{deps: deps, cpID: counterpartyID, loadErr: err}
    }
    items, err := deps.Store.WaterBalloons().ListConversation(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID, 200)
    if err == nil {
        // Mark only the inbound side as read. This is the new mark-read trigger.
        _ = deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkConversationRead(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID)
        // Reflect the mark in our local copy so View doesn't render stale "NEW".
        now := time.Now()
        for _, w := range items {
            if w.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !w.ReadAt.Valid {
                w.ReadAt = sql.NullTime{Time: now, Valid: true}
            }
        }
    }
    return wbThreadModel{
        deps:    deps,
        cpID:    counterpartyID,
        cpUserID: cp.UserID,
        items:   items,
        loadErr: err,
    }
}
```

Mirrors `mailThreadModel`'s mark-read-on-open pattern (`screen_mail.go:149-162`).

### 3b. Update — keys

```go
case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
    return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
case "Q":
    return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
case "up", "k":
    if m.scroll > 0 { m.scroll-- }
case "down", "j":
    m.scroll++ // clamped at render time
case "g", "home":
    m.scroll = 0
case "G", "end":
    m.scroll = m.maxScroll() // helper using len(items) and visible height
case "c", "r":
    return m, func() tea.Msg {
        return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: m.cpUserID}
    }
```

`c` and `r` both go to compose pre-filled. `r` because muscle memory from the inbox; `c` for symmetry with the rest of the app. **Phase 2 will replace this** with an inline textinput, but the keymap is forward-compatible: Phase 2 keeps the same keys, they just toggle focus rather than navigate away.

### 3c. Update — live append (recommendation: include in Phase 1)

**Recommendation: include the minimum hook now.** It's load-bearing for Phase 2, and it's small (~15 lines). It also fixes a subtle UX bug: today, if alice opens her thread with bob and bob sends a WB while alice has the thread open, alice sees the toast (good) but the thread doesn't update (bad — she has to back out and re-enter).

```go
case WBIncomingMsg:
    if !m.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
        return m, nil
    }
    ctx := context.Background()
    if wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().GetByID(ctx, msg.ID); err == nil {
        m.items = append(m.items, wb)
    }
    // Mark read since it's now visible on screen.
    _ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, msg.ID)
    return m, nil
```

This requires:
- A new `WaterBalloonRepo.GetByID(ctx, id) (*WaterBalloon, error)` (one-liner using `wbColumns`). Add it to §1 alongside the others.
- A `(m wbThreadModel) matchesCounterparty(handle string) bool` that compares to `m.cpUserID` case-insensitively. (`equalFold` from `chat/broker.go:142` could move up to a shared util — or just inline `strings.EqualFold`.)

**Toast suppression deferred to Phase 2.** Today's behavior — toast fires, thread also appends — is fine for Phase 1; the user gets visible confirmation. Phase 2 will add a "is the active screen a matching thread?" check in `Root.Update` to suppress duplicate toasts.

### 3d. View

Chat-style scrollback. Each item:

```
  alice  2026-05-06 14:23
    hi how's it going
  
  you    2026-05-06 14:24
    pretty good thanks
```

Sender label uses `StyleHighlight` for messages from the counterparty (incoming) and `StyleDim` for outgoing. Wrap body to width-4 (or so), one message per "card". Use a viewport-style render: skip the first `m.scroll` lines, render up to `m.height-4` lines after that.

## 4. Routing: `internal/tui/messages.go` and `root.go`

### 4a. New screen constant

In `messages.go:6-24` (after `ScreenWBCompose` and before `ScreenOnline` to keep WB screens contiguous):

### 4b. NavigateMsg field — recommendation: add a new field

**Recommendation: add `CounterpartyUserID int64` rather than reuse `Recipient`.**

`Recipient` is documented (`messages.go:32`) as "prefilled recipient userid" — a *string handle* used by compose screens. The thread screen needs the *numeric ID* for the SQL queries. Reusing `Recipient` would force every navigate site to do a `Users().GetByUserID` lookup just to pass it back. Adding a field is one line.

```go
CounterpartyUserID int64 // for ScreenWBThread: which DM thread to open
```

(Yes, `MailThreadID` is also `int64` and feels parallel, but it points to a `mail.thread_id`, a different concept. Don't conflate.)

```

Place it next to `ScreenWBInbox` / `ScreenWBCompose` for grouping.

- `TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_Grouping` — alice has 3 with bob, 2 with carol; expect 2 rows, in `LastAt DESC` order.

Critical: don't accidentally mark messages alice *sent* as read (they're already read by definition, but the `to_user_id` filter must hold or we'd be writing to wrong rows).

## 7. Verification (manual, two SSH sessions)

1. Start server: `make run` (or whichever target wraps `./sshbbs`).
2. Terminal A: `ssh alice@localhost -p 2222`. Terminal B: `ssh bob@localhost -p 2222`.
3. **Build history while both online:**
   - In B: Ctrl+U (open inbox) → `c` (compose) → To: `alice`, Body: `hi from bob`. Ctrl+S.
   - In A: see toast `💧 bob: hi from bob`. (Already auto-marked read because online.)
   - In A: Ctrl+U → `c` → To: `bob`, Body: `hey bob`. Ctrl+S.
   - Repeat with body `another` to give the thread depth.
4. **Build offline unread:**
   - Disconnect A.
   - In B: Ctrl+U → `c` → To: `alice`, Body: `bob unread 1`. Repeat with `bob unread 2`.
5. **Reconnect alice:**
   - `ssh alice@localhost -p 2222`. The drain-on-connect (`server.go:138-148`) replays both messages as toasts, marking them read **and** keeping them in the conversation history.
6. **Phase 1 inbox:** Alice presses Ctrl+U. Expect: one row, `bob`, unread `0` (drain already cleared them), preview shows the most recent body. *If we instead want fresh-on-reconnect to show as unread, we'd suppress the immediate `MarkRead` in `server.go:146` and rely on the thread to mark them — but that's a separate decision; out of scope for Phase 1.*
7. **Drill into thread:** Alice presses Enter on the bob row. Expect: scrollback showing all 5 messages in chronological order, sender prefix correct (you/bob), body wraps reasonably, scroll with j/k.
8. **Read-marking verification:** Quit and reconnect alice — no toasts replay (DB has them all read).
9. **Live append with thread open:** Alice in thread with bob. Bob sends `live test`. Expect toast PLUS the row appearing at the bottom of the thread without re-entering. Run `sqlite3 sshbbs.db "SELECT id, body, read_at FROM water_balloons ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5"` and confirm the new row's `read_at` is set.
10. **Counterparty roll-up correctness:** Have a third user `carol` send to alice. Alice's inbox should now show 2 rows, carol on top (unread first), bob below (all read).
11. **Self-WB sanity check:** Alice tries `c` → To: `alice`. See concern #1 below — current behavior is "succeeds and shows up in your own inbox as a row from yourself." Note this; don't fix in Phase 1.

## 8. Concerns / edge cases

1. **Self-water-balloon is not blocked.** `screen_wb.go:234` does `GetByUserID(to)` — if `to == m.deps.User.UserID` it succeeds. Then `Insert` writes `from_user_id == to_user_id`. With Phase 1's CTE, the `cp_id` for that row is `to_user_id` (which equals viewer's own ID) — alice will see a "conversation with herself" row. Mostly harmless but weird. **Recommendation:** out of Phase 1 scope; add a one-line guard in `wbComposeModel.submit` later (`if target.ID == from.ID { m.err = "cannot send to yourself"; return m, nil }`). Same applies to mail (out of scope but worth a TODO).

2. **Threading with deleted users.** No user-delete code exists today (`grep -rn "DELETE FROM users"` returns nothing). The `to_user_id` and `from_user_id` columns have `REFERENCES users(id)` but no cascade. When delete eventually lands, the JOIN to `users` in `ListCounterpartiesFor` would silently drop those conversations from the inbox — surprising but not corrupt. Out of Phase 1 scope; if/when delete lands, switch to soft-delete or LEFT JOIN with a fallback handle. Worth a TODO comment in the new SQL.

3. **Renamed counterpart / `from_userid` denorm.** The `from_userid` column on `water_balloons` is a snapshot of the sender's handle at insert time. If alice renames to `alice2`, old rows still say `from_userid='alice'`. The `ListCounterpartiesFor` query JOINs `users` on the canonical id, so the inbox header always shows the *current* handle — good. But the *body* of `ListConversation` still returns old `FromUserIDStr` from the row itself, so the in-thread "alice: hi" prefix would say the old name. **Recommendation:** in the thread `View`, derive the prefix from `m.cpUserID` (current handle) for incoming and "you" for outgoing — don't trust `w.FromUserIDStr`. This is clean and free.

4. **Empty conversation case in `ListConversation`.** If you somehow open a thread for a counterparty with whom you have zero messages (shouldn't happen via the normal flow because the inbox only lists rows that exist), the screen should show an "(empty thread)" placeholder rather than blowing up. Mirror `screen_mail.go:220-223`.

5. **Concurrency on `MarkConversationRead`.** Holds `writeMu` correctly. But the live-append path in §3c also calls `MarkRead` — if a second `WBIncomingMsg` arrives while the constructor is still running its `MarkConversationRead`, both calls take the mutex, no race. Tests should run with `-race` (already standard, per the conventions section).

6. **`CounterpartyUserID` zero check in `Root.navigate`.** If a buggy caller emits `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: 0}`, the constructor will get `users.GetByID(0)` → `ErrUserNotFound`. The screen will show `loadErr` — acceptable, not a crash. Don't bother adding a guard.

7. **`unread_count` overflow in column rendering.** Counterparty unread is `int64`. If we ever have a chatty user with > 999 unread the column padding (`statusW=5`) handles it. No-op for now.

## 9. Sequencing (suggested)

Pure additive then surgical: each step compiles and tests should pass between steps, so a reviewer can land in pieces.

1. **Repo + tests first.** Add `WBCounterparty`, `ListCounterpartiesFor`, `ListConversation`, `MarkConversationRead`, `GetByID`. Land the repo tests. (No screen wired up yet — existing inbox still uses `ListInboxFor`. Build is green.)
2. **NavigateMsg field + screen constant.** `CounterpartyUserID` and `ScreenWBThread` added. Routing case in `root.go` returns the model. Compiles, untouched runtime behavior.
3. **Thread screen + tests.** `wbThreadModel` and `screen_wb_test.go`'s thread tests. (Inbox still flat.)
4. **Inbox swap + tests.** Replace flat-list inbox with counterparty inbox. Remove `MarkAllReadFor` line. Update inbox tests (rename, change expectations).
5. **Live append in thread + test.** The `WBIncomingMsg` case in `wbThreadModel.Update`.
6. **Optional online-list `t` shortcut.** One Update case + help-text update + one test.

## Critical files for implementation

- /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/waterballoons.go
- /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb.go
- /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/messages.go

(Secondary: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_online.go` for the optional `t` shortcut, and a new `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_wb_test.go` for TUI-side tests.)

Plan a two-phase feature delivery for /Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs (PTT-style BBS over SSH, charmbracelet/wish + bubbletea + SQLite). User's intent: "make 水球 (water balloon) into chat-history-with-quick-reply-shortcut" — meaning eventually 水球 = a quick way to drop one line into a 1-to-1 DM with another user. We've agreed on two phases.

## Confirmed scope (from user)

- **Phase 1**: Conversation **thread** view. Group inbox by counterparty, drill in to see both-direction history with that user (read + unread, time-ordered).
- **Phase 2**: DM-style chat screen. Same screen layout but with a textinput at the bottom + live append when counterparty sends. 水球 toast still fires for off-screen messages.
- **Read-marking change** (applies to Phase 1): Currently `wbInboxModel` constructor calls `MarkAllReadFor` on open (`screen_wb.go:34`) — that's "ack everything by opening the inbox". User wants this **removed**: read should be marked **only when entering a specific user's thread**.

## Confirmed groundwork (already exists)

- `water_balloons` table is the durable store. Schema (`internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql:57-68`): `id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live, read_at, created_at`. No schema change needed for either phase.
- Repo: `internal/store/waterballoons.go`. Existing methods: `Insert`, `ListUnreadFor` (offline-replay), `ListInboxFor` (recipient-only flat list), `MarkRead`, `MarkAllReadFor`.
- Inbox screen: `internal/tui/screen_wb.go` lines 20-124 (`wbInboxModel`). Compose: lines 126-291 (`wbComposeModel`).
- Broker (`internal/chat/broker.go`): `Send(toUID, msg)` for 1-to-1, `SendToAll(excludeUID, msg)` broadcast. Send returns `bool` (true if recipient online). `WBIncomingMsg` is the live-delivery message type.
- Toast/live receive: `Root.Update` handles `WBIncomingMsg` at `internal/tui/root.go:89-97`, calls `MarkRead(ID)` immediately on receive (which only fires for currently-online recipients via Broker.Send).
- Reconnect replay: `internal/server/server.go` "Drain unread" section — sends each unread `WBIncomingMsg` to the program on connect.
- Screen routing: `Root.navigate` switches on `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenXxx}`. Screen constants and message types are in `internal/tui/messages.go`. Window-resize forwarded to active sub before Root logic; on navigate, last `WindowSizeMsg` is resent to the new sub.
- Conventions: vim keys (h/j/k/l) work alongside arrows on lists; Esc/Backspace go back. Form screens (compose) skip h/l. UTF-8 throughout; CJK width via `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers (`PadRight`, `Truncate`, `Width`).
- Concurrency: `Store.writeMu` on multi-statement repo writes. Tests under `-race`. `storetest.New(t)` for fresh per-test DB.
- Online list screen (`screen_online`) already has a "send WB to selected user" entry point — we may want to also offer "open thread with X".

## Phase 1 design questions to answer

1. **Repo additions** to `internal/store/waterballoons.go`:
   - `ListConversation(ctx, userA, userB int64, limit int) ([]*WaterBalloon, error)` — both directions between two users, time-ordered ASC. Index check: `idx_wb_to_unread (to_user_id, read_at)` exists; do we need a new composite index for the two-way query? Evaluate vs. dataset size (small-scale BBS, can be just WHERE clause without new index initially).
   - `ListCounterpartiesFor(ctx, userID int64, limit int)` — returns one row per counterparty, with `(counterparty_user_id, counterparty_userid_str, last_msg_at, unread_count)`. Single SQL query using GROUP BY on a derived counterparty column (`CASE WHEN from_user_id = ? THEN to_user_id ELSE from_user_id END`).
   - `MarkConversationRead(ctx, viewerID, counterpartyID int64) error` — `UPDATE … WHERE to_user_id = viewerID AND from_user_id = counterpartyID AND read_at IS NULL`.
2. **Inbox screen rework** (`screen_wb.go` `wbInboxModel`):
   - Replace `ListInboxFor` call with `ListCounterpartiesFor`.
   - Remove the `MarkAllReadFor` call from constructor (line 34) — no auto-mark-read on inbox open.
   - View: list of counterparties with `(name, unread_count, last_msg_at)`.
   - Enter/r/Right/l drills into thread (new screen). Keep `c` for compose.
3. **New thread screen** `wbThreadModel` (likely in `screen_wb.go` for cohesion, or a new `screen_wb_thread.go`):
   - Constructor takes the counterparty user (look up by ID or userid string — decide which).
   - On mount: call `ListConversation` then `MarkConversationRead`.
   - View: scrollback of messages, "you" vs "alice" prefix, time stamp, NEW marker stripped after mark-read.
   - Keys: j/k/up/down scroll, Esc/Backspace/h/Left back to inbox, c to compose to this user (pre-fills recipient — same path as inbox `r`), Enter selects (no-op for now, hook for Phase 2).
4. **Routing**: Add `ScreenWBThread` constant in `internal/tui/messages.go`. Add case in `Root.navigate` that constructs `wbThreadModel` with the counterparty. The `NavigateMsg` will need to carry the counterparty user ID/string — currently it has `Recipient` for compose; we may want a separate `CounterpartyUserIDStr` or just reuse `Recipient`.
5. **Live append while in thread** (decide for Phase 1 or defer to Phase 2):

6. **Tests**:

## Phase 2 design (sketch only — final plan focuses on Phase 1)

- Augment `wbThreadModel` with a textinput at the bottom; Tab to switch focus between scrollback (j/k) and input.
- On Enter (in input): call `WaterBalloons().Insert` and `Broker.Send` (same path as `wbComposeModel.submit`). Append the row to local scrollback immediately on success. Clear input.
- Toast suppression: when `Root` receives `WBIncomingMsg` and the active screen is `wbThreadModel` with matching counterparty, skip the toast (it'll be appended in the thread anyway).
- Online-list screen entry point: `r`/Enter opens thread with selected user instead of (or in addition to) compose.

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	type WaterBalloon struct {
10		ID            int64
11		FromUserID    int64
12		FromUserIDStr string
13		ToUserID      int64
14		Body          string
15		DeliveredLive bool
16		ReadAt        sql.NullTime
17		CreatedAt     time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type WaterBalloonRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	const wbColumns = `id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live, read_at, created_at`
23	
24	func scanWB(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
25		w := &WaterBalloon{}
26		var deliveredLive int64
27		if err := row.Scan(&w.ID, &w.FromUserID, &w.FromUserIDStr, &w.ToUserID, &w.Body, &deliveredLive, &w.ReadAt, &w.CreatedAt); err != nil {
28			return nil, err
29		}
30		w.DeliveredLive = deliveredLive != 0
31		return w, nil
32	}
33	
34	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, body string, deliveredLive bool) (*WaterBalloon, error) {
35		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
36		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
37		live := int64(0)
38		if deliveredLive {
39			live = 1
40		}
41		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
42			`INSERT INTO water_balloons (from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
43			fromID, fromUserID, toID, body, live,
44		)
45		if err != nil {
46			return nil, err
47		}
48		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
49		if err != nil {
50			return nil, err
51		}
52		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE id = ?`, id)
53		return scanWB(row)
54	}
55	
56	// ListUnreadFor returns unread water balloons for the recipient, oldest
57	// first, so the TUI can replay them on reconnect.
58	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListUnreadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
59		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
60			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL ORDER BY id ASC`,
61			toUserID,
62		)
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66		defer rows.Close()
67		var out []*WaterBalloon
68		for rows.Next() {
69			w, err := scanWB(rows)
70			if err != nil {
71				return nil, err
72			}
73			out = append(out, w)
74		}
75		return out, rows.Err()
76	}
77	
78	// ListInboxFor returns the recipient's inbox: unread first (newest unread
79	// up top), then read messages newest-first.
80	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) ListInboxFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64, limit int) ([]*WaterBalloon, error) {
81		if limit <= 0 {
82			limit = 50
83		}
84		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
85			`SELECT `+wbColumns+` FROM water_balloons WHERE to_user_id = ?
86			 ORDER BY (read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
87			toUserID, limit,
88		)
89		if err != nil {
90			return nil, err
91		}
92		defer rows.Close()
93		var out []*WaterBalloon
94		for rows.Next() {
95			w, err := scanWB(rows)
96			if err != nil {
97				return nil, err
98			}
99			out = append(out, w)
100		}
101		return out, rows.Err()
102	}
103	
104	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkRead(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
105		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
106		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
107		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
108			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, id,
109		)
110		return err
111	}
112	
113	func (r *WaterBalloonRepo) MarkAllReadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) error {
114		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
115		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
116		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
117			`UPDATE water_balloons SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`,
118			toUserID,
119		)
120		return err
121	}
122
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"

9		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"

13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
14	)

16	// =====================================================================
17	// Inbox
18	// =====================================================================
19	
20	type wbInboxModel struct {
21		deps    Deps
22		items   []*store.WaterBalloon
23		cursor  int
24		width   int
25		height  int
26		loadErr error
27	}
28	
29	func newWBInboxModel(deps Deps) wbInboxModel {
30		items, err := deps.Store.WaterBalloons().ListInboxFor(context.Background(), deps.User.ID, 100)
31		// Mark all unread as read once shown — matches PTT behaviour where
32		// reading the inbox clears the "new mail" indicator.
33		if err == nil {
34			_ = deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkAllReadFor(context.Background(), deps.User.ID)
35		}
36		return wbInboxModel{deps: deps, items: items, loadErr: err}
37	}
38	
39	func (m wbInboxModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
40	
41	func (m wbInboxModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
42		switch msg := msg.(type) {
43		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
44			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
45			return m, nil
46		case tea.KeyMsg:
47			switch msg.String() {
48			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
49				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
50			case "up", "k":
51				if m.cursor > 0 {
52					m.cursor--
53				}
54			case "down", "j":
55				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
56					m.cursor++
57				}
58			case "c":
59				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose} }
60			case "r", "enter", " ", "right", "l":
61				if len(m.items) == 0 {
62					return m, nil
63				}
64				it := m.items[m.cursor]
65				return m, func() tea.Msg {
66					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: it.FromUserIDStr}
67				}
68			}
69		}
70		return m, nil
71	}
72	
73	func (m wbInboxModel) View() string {
74		var b strings.Builder
75		b.WriteString("\n")
76		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 水球 Water Balloons "))
77		b.WriteString("\n\n")
78	
79		if m.loadErr != nil {
80			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
81			return b.String()
82		}
83		if len(m.items) == 0 {
84			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no messages — press c to compose)") + "\n")
85			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("c compose · Esc/←/h back"))
86			return b.String()
87		}
88	
89		const (
90			dateW   = 16
91			fromW   = 14
92			statusW = 5
93		)
94		bodyW := max(20, m.width-2-dateW-1-fromW-1-statusW-3)
95		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",
96			PadRight("Date", dateW),
97			PadRight("From", fromW),
98			PadRight("•", statusW),
99			PadRight("Body", bodyW),
100		)
101		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
102	
103		for i, it := range m.items {
104			status := "·"
105			if !it.ReadAt.Valid {
106				status = "NEW"
107			}
108			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",
109				PadRight(it.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"), dateW),
110				PadRight(it.FromUserIDStr, fromW),
111				PadRight(status, statusW),
112				Truncate(it.Body, bodyW),
113			)
114			if i == m.cursor {
115				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
116			} else {
117				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
118			}
119		}
120	
121		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l/r reply · c compose · Esc/←/h back"))
122		b.WriteString("\n")
123		return b.String()
124	}
125	
126	// =====================================================================
127	// Compose
128	// =====================================================================
129	
130	const (
131		wbFocusTo = iota
132		wbFocusBody
133	)
134	
135	type wbComposeModel struct {
136		deps   Deps
137		to     textinput.Model
138		body   textarea.Model
139		focus  int
140		width  int
141		height int
142		err    string
143		sent   string // success: recipient userid
144	}
145	
146	func newWBComposeModel(deps Deps, recipient string) wbComposeModel {
147		to := textinput.New()
148		to.Placeholder = "alice"
149		to.CharLimit = 12
150		to.Width = 30
151		to.SetValue(recipient)
152	
153		body := textarea.New()
154		body.Placeholder = "your one-line message…"
155		body.CharLimit = 240
156		body.SetWidth(60)
157		body.SetHeight(4)
158	
159		m := wbComposeModel{deps: deps, to: to, body: body}
160		if recipient == "" {
161			m.focus = wbFocusTo
162			m.to.Focus()
163		} else {
164			m.focus = wbFocusBody
165			m.body.Focus()
166		}
167		return m
168	}
169	
170	func (m wbComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
171	
172	func (m wbComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
173		switch msg := msg.(type) {
174		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
175			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
176			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-12))
177			return m, nil
178	
179		case tea.KeyMsg:
180			if m.sent != "" {
181				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
182			}
183			switch msg.String() {
184			case "esc":
185				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
186			case "tab":
187				m.toggleFocus()
188				return m, nil
189			case "ctrl+s":
190				return m.submit()
191			}
192	
193			if m.focus == wbFocusTo {
194				if k := msg.String(); k == "enter" {
195					m.toggleFocus()
196					return m, nil
197				}
198				var cmd tea.Cmd
199				m.to, cmd = m.to.Update(msg)
200				return m, cmd
201			}
202			var cmd tea.Cmd
203			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
204			return m, cmd
205		}
206		return m, nil
207	}
208	
209	func (m *wbComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
210		if m.focus == wbFocusTo {
211			m.focus = wbFocusBody
212			m.to.Blur()
213			m.body.Focus()
214		} else {
215			m.focus = wbFocusTo
216			m.body.Blur()
217			m.to.Focus()
218		}
219	}
220	
221	func (m wbComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
222		to := strings.TrimSpace(m.to.Value())
223		body := strings.TrimSpace(m.body.Value())
224		if to == "" {
225			m.err = "recipient required"
226			return m, nil
227		}
228		if body == "" {
229			m.err = "body required"
230			return m, nil
231		}
232	
233		ctx := context.Background()
234		target, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, to)
235		if err != nil {
236			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
237				m.err = "no such user: " + to
238			} else {
239				m.err = err.Error()
240			}
241			return m, nil
242		}
243		from := m.deps.User
244	
245		delivered := false
246		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
247			// Persist first, then notify with the canonical row's ID.
248		}
249		wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, target.ID, body, false)
250		if err != nil {
251			m.err = err.Error()
252			return m, nil
253		}
254		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
255			delivered = m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, WBIncomingMsg{
256				ID:         wb.ID,
257				FromUserID: from.UserID,
258				Body:       body,
259			})
260			if delivered {
261				// The recipient saw it live, so we can mark it read immediately.
262				_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID)
263			}
264		}
265		m.sent = target.UserID
266		return m, nil
267	}
268	
269	func (m wbComposeModel) View() string {
270		var b strings.Builder
271		b.WriteString("\n")
272		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 丟水球 Send Water Balloon "))
273		b.WriteString("\n\n")
274	
275		if m.sent != "" {
276			b.WriteString("  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ sent to "+m.sent) + "\n\n")
277			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("any key returns to inbox"))
278			return b.String()
279		}
280	
281		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("To (userid):") + "\n")
282		b.WriteString("  " + m.to.View() + "\n\n")
283		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
284		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
285	
286		if m.err != "" {
287			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
288		}
289		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in To) → body · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
290		return b.String()
291	}
292

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/messages.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenOnline
15		ScreenMailInbox
16		ScreenMailThread
17		ScreenMailCompose
18		ScreenPasswordChange
19		ScreenAdminUsers
20		ScreenArticleEdit
21		ScreenArticleExport
22		ScreenBoardSplash
23		ScreenBoardBannerEdit
24	)
25	
26	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
27	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
28	type NavigateMsg struct {
29		To           Screen
30		BoardID      int64
31		ArticleID    int64
32		Recipient    string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
33		MailID       int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
34		MailThreadID int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
35	}
36	
37	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
38	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
39	
40	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
41	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
42	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
43		ID         int64
44		FromUserID string
45		Body       string
46	}
47	
48	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
49	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
50	// re-render.
51	type PushAddedMsg struct {
52		ArticleID  int64
53		UserUserID string
54		Kind       string
55		Body       string
56	}
57	
58	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
59	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
60	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
61	// counter from DB.
62	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
63		ID         int64
64		ThreadID   int64
65		FromUserID string
66		Subject    string
67	}
68	
69	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
70	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
71	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
72	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
73		BoardID      int64
74		ArticleID    int64
75		AuthorUserID string
76		Title        string
77	}
78	
79	// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
80	// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
81	// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
82	type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
83		ArticleID int64
84	}
85	
86	// ArticlePinChangedMsg is broadcast when a mod pins or unpins an article.
87	// Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article list so the
88	// pinned-first ordering and the [M] marker stay current across all live
89	// sessions viewing the same board. ArticleID + Pinned are advisory; the
90	// re-fetch is the source of truth.
91	type ArticlePinChangedMsg struct {
92		BoardID   int64
93		ArticleID int64
94		Pinned    bool
95	}
96
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/root.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
15	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
16	type Deps struct {
17		Store              *store.Store
18		Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
19		User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
20		IsRegister         bool
21		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
25	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
26	// and the toast footer.
27	type Root struct {
28		deps   Deps
29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	}
36	
37	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
38	
39	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
40	//
41	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
42	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
43	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
44	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
45		r := Root{deps: deps}
46		switch {
47		case deps.IsRegister:
48			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
49		case deps.MustChangePassword:
50			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
51		default:
52			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
53		}
54		return r
55	}
56	
57	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
58		return m.sub.Init()
59	}
60	
61	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
62		switch msg := msg.(type) {
63		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
64			m.width = msg.Width
65			m.height = msg.Height
66			var cmd tea.Cmd
67			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
68			return m, cmd
69	
70		case tea.KeyMsg:
71			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
72			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			}
75			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
76			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
77				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
78			}
79	
80		case NavigateMsg:
81			return m.navigate(msg)
82	
83		case ErrorMsg:
84			if msg.Err != nil {
85				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
86			}
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case WBIncomingMsg:
90			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
91			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
92			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
93				go func(id int64) {
94					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
95				}(msg.ID)
96			}
97			// fall through so the active screen can also react
98		}
99	
100		var cmd tea.Cmd
101		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
102		return m, cmd
103	}
104	
105	func (m Root) View() string {
106		v := m.sub.View()
107		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
108			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
109		}
110		return v
111	}
112	
113	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
114		m.toast = s
115		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
116	}
117	
118	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
119	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
120	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
121		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
122			return nil
123		}
124		switch to {
125		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
126			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
132	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
133	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
134		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
135			return nil
136		}
137		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
138			return nil
139		}
140		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
141	}
142	
143	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
144		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
145			return m, cmd
146		}
147		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
148			return m, cmd
149		}
150		var sub tea.Model
151		switch n.To {
152		case ScreenMainMenu:
153			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
154		case ScreenBoardList:
155			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
156		case ScreenBoardView:
157			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
158		case ScreenArticleView:
159			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
160		case ScreenPostCompose:
161			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
162		case ScreenWBInbox:
163			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
164		case ScreenWBCompose:
165			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
166		case ScreenOnline:
167			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
168		case ScreenMailInbox:
169			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
170		case ScreenMailThread:
171			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
172		case ScreenMailCompose:
173			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
174		case ScreenPasswordChange:
175			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
176		case ScreenAdminUsers:
177			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
178		case ScreenArticleEdit:
179			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
180		case ScreenArticleExport:
181			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
182		case ScreenBoardSplash:
183			sub = newBoardSplashModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
184		case ScreenBoardBannerEdit:
185			sub = newBoardBannerEditModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
186		default:
187			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
188			return m, nil
189		}
190		m.sub = sub
191		cmd := sub.Init()
192		if m.width > 0 {
193			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
194			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
195			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
196			if cmd2 != nil {
197				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
198			}
199		}
200		return m, cmd
201	}
202
