 `./internal/store/waterballoons_test.go`

```text
1	package store_test
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
8	)
9	
10	func TestWaterBalloons_InsertAndList(t *testing.T) {
11		ctx := context.Background()
12		st := storetest.New(t)
13		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
14		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
15	
16		for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
17			_, err := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "msg", false)
18			if err != nil {
19				t.Fatalf("Insert: %v", err)
20			}
21		}
22	
23		unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(ctx, bob.ID)
24		if err != nil {
25			t.Fatalf("ListUnreadFor: %v", err)
26		}
27		if len(unread) != 3 {
28			t.Errorf("got %d unread, want 3", len(unread))
29		}
30		// Oldest first by id ASC — important for replay ordering.
31		for i := 0; i < len(unread)-1; i++ {
32			if unread[i].ID > unread[i+1].ID {
33				t.Errorf("ListUnreadFor not in id ASC order")
34			}
35		}
36	}
37	
38	func TestWaterBalloons_MarkRead(t *testing.T) {
39		ctx := context.Background()
40		st := storetest.New(t)
41		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
42		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
43		wb, err := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "hi", true)
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("Insert: %v", err)
46		}
47		if err := st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, wb.ID); err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("MarkRead: %v", err)
49		}
50		got, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(ctx, bob.ID)
51		if err != nil {
52			t.Fatalf("ListUnreadFor: %v", err)
53		}
54		if len(got) != 0 {
55			t.Errorf("got %d unread after MarkRead, want 0", len(got))
56		}
57	}
58	
59	func TestWaterBalloons_MarkAllReadFor(t *testing.T) {
60		ctx := context.Background()
61		st := storetest.New(t)
62		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
63		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
64		for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
65			_, _ = st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "msg", false)
66		}
67		if err := st.WaterBalloons().MarkAllReadFor(ctx, bob.ID); err != nil {
68			t.Fatalf("MarkAllReadFor: %v", err)
69		}
70		unread, _ := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(ctx, bob.ID)
71		if len(unread) != 0 {
72			t.Errorf("after MarkAllReadFor: %d unread, want 0", len(unread))
73		}
74	}
75	
76	// TestWaterBalloons_InboxOrdering checks that ListInboxFor returns unread
77	// before read, and within each group newest-first by id.
78	func TestWaterBalloons_InboxOrdering(t *testing.T) {
79		ctx := context.Background()
80		st := storetest.New(t)
81		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
82		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
83	
84		// Insert 4 in order, mark the second one read.
85		w1, _ := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "first",  false)
86		w2, _ := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "second", false)
87		w3, _ := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "third",  false)
88		w4, _ := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "fourth", false)
89		_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, w2.ID)
90		_ = w1
91		_ = w3
92		_ = w4
93	
94		got, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListInboxFor(ctx, bob.ID, 0)
95		if err != nil {
96			t.Fatalf("ListInboxFor: %v", err)
97		}
98		if len(got) != 4 {
99			t.Fatalf("got %d, want 4", len(got))
100		}
101		// Expected order: unread newest-first (w4, w3, w1), then read (w2).
102		wantBodies := []string{"fourth", "third", "first", "second"}
103		for i, w := range wantBodies {
104			if got[i].Body != w {
105				t.Errorf("[%d].Body = %q, want %q", i, got[i].Body, w)
106			}
107		}
108	}
109	
110	func TestWaterBalloons_DeliveredLiveFlag(t *testing.T) {
111		ctx := context.Background()
112		st := storetest.New(t)
113		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
114		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
115	
116		live, _ := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "live", true)
117		offline, _ := st.WaterBalloons().Insert(ctx, alice.ID, alice.UserID, bob.ID, "offline", false)
118		if !live.DeliveredLive {
119			t.Error("live WB has DeliveredLive=false")
120		}
121		if offline.DeliveredLive {
122			t.Error("offline WB has DeliveredLive=true")
123		}
124	}
```

 `./internal/chat/broker.go`

```text
1	// Package chat is the in-memory hub that connects live SSH sessions for
2	// real-time features (water balloons, push broadcasts). It mirrors PTT's
3	// `userinfo_t` shared-memory table conceptually, but as a process-local
4	// map[user_id][]*Session — no SHM, no daemons.
5	package chat
6	
7	import (
8		"sync"
9	
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	)
12	
13	// Sender is the minimal slice of *tea.Program the broker actually needs.
14	// Decoupling lets tests substitute a recording fake without spinning up a
15	// real bubbletea program.
16	type Sender interface {
17		Send(tea.Msg)
18	}
19	
20	// Session is one live SSH connection. The same user may have multiple
21	// Sessions (PTT calls this "雙開"); the broker keeps them all and
22	// broadcasts to each.
23	type Session struct {
24		UserID    int64
25		UserIDStr string
26		Program   Sender
27	}
28	
29	// OnlineUser is the externally-visible view of a logged-in user.
30	type OnlineUser struct {
31		UserID    int64
32		UserIDStr string
33		Sessions  int
34	}
35	
36	type Broker struct {
37		mu       sync.RWMutex
38		sessions map[int64][]*Session
39	}
40	
41	func NewBroker() *Broker {
42		return &Broker{sessions: make(map[int64][]*Session)}
43	}
44	
45	func (b *Broker) Register(s *Session) {
46		b.mu.Lock()
47		defer b.mu.Unlock()
48		b.sessions[s.UserID] = append(b.sessions[s.UserID], s)
49	}
50	
51	// Unregister removes a single session for the given user (matched by
52	// Sender identity). If it was the last session, the user disappears from
53	// the online list.
54	func (b *Broker) Unregister(userID int64, prog Sender) {
55		b.mu.Lock()
56		defer b.mu.Unlock()
57		list := b.sessions[userID]
58		for i, s := range list {
59			if s.Program == prog {
60				b.sessions[userID] = append(list[:i], list[i+1:]...)
61				break
62			}
63		}
64		if len(b.sessions[userID]) == 0 {
65			delete(b.sessions, userID)
66		}
67	}
68	
69	// Send delivers msg to every live session of toUID. Returns true if at
70	// least one session received it (i.e. the recipient is online).
71	func (b *Broker) Send(toUID int64, msg tea.Msg) bool {
72		b.mu.RLock()
73		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
74		list := b.sessions[toUID]
75		for _, s := range list {
76			s.Program.Send(msg)
77		}
78		return len(list) > 0
79	}
80	
81	// SendToAll broadcasts to every online session. excludeUID lets the
82	// originator skip themselves (pass 0 to broadcast to everyone).
83	func (b *Broker) SendToAll(excludeUID int64, msg tea.Msg) {
84		b.mu.RLock()
85		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
86		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
87			if uid == excludeUID {
88				continue
89			}
90			for _, s := range list {
91				s.Program.Send(msg)
92			}
93		}
94	}
95	
96	// LookupByUserID returns the numeric user_id for a logged-in account name,
97	// or 0 if nobody by that name is currently online. Case-insensitive.
98	func (b *Broker) LookupByUserID(name string) int64 {
99		b.mu.RLock()
100		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
101		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
102			for _, s := range list {
103				if equalFold(s.UserIDStr, name) {
104					return uid
105				}
106			}
107		}
108		return 0
109	}
110	
111	func (b *Broker) OnlineList() []OnlineUser {
112		b.mu.RLock()
113		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
114		out := make([]OnlineUser, 0, len(b.sessions))
115		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
116			if len(list) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			out = append(out, OnlineUser{
120				UserID:    uid,
121				UserIDStr: list[0].UserIDStr,
122				Sessions:  len(list),
123			})
124		}
125		return out
126	}
127	
128	// SessionsSnapshot returns a defensive copy of every session pointer for
129	// graceful shutdown (so the caller can iterate without holding the lock
130	// while blocking on per-program quits).
131	func (b *Broker) SessionsSnapshot() []*Session {
132		b.mu.RLock()
133		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
134		var out []*Session
135		for _, list := range b.sessions {
136			out = append(out, list...)
137		}
138		return out
139	}
140	
141	// equalFold is strings.EqualFold inlined to avoid the import in this tiny package.
142	func equalFold(a, b string) bool {
143		if len(a) != len(b) {
144			return false
145		}
146		for i := 0; i < len(a); i++ {
147			ca, cb := a[i], b[i]
148			if ca >= 'A' && ca <= 'Z' {
149				ca += 32
150			}
151			if cb >= 'A' && cb <= 'Z' {
152				cb += 32
153			}
154			if ca != cb {
155				return false
156			}
157		}
158		return true
159	}
160
```

 `./internal/server/server.go`

```text
1	package server
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6	
7		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/log"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish"
11		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/activeterm"
12		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/bubbletea"
13		"github.com/charmbracelet/wish/logging"
14		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
15		gossh "golang.org/x/crypto/ssh"
16	
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui"
21	)
22	
23	type Config struct {
24		Addr    string
25		HostKey string
26	}
27	
28	func New(cfg Config, st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) (*ssh.Server, error) {
29		return wish.NewServer(
30			wish.WithAddress(cfg.Addr),
31			wish.WithHostKeyPath(cfg.HostKey),
32			wish.WithPasswordAuth(PasswordAuth(st)),
33			withReservedNoneAuth(),
34			wish.WithMiddleware(
35				bubbletea.MiddlewareWithProgramHandler(makeProgramHandler(st, broker), termenv.ANSI256),
36				activeterm.Middleware(),
37				logging.Middleware(),
38			),
39		)
40	}
41	
42	// withReservedNoneAuth enables SSH "none" authentication for the two
43	// reserved usernames where a password is meaningless: `guest` (read-only
44	// spectator) and `new` (in-TUI registration). Other usernames still go
45	// through the password callback.
46	//
47	// charmbracelet/ssh only sets NoClientAuth=true when no other handlers
48	// are configured (see its server.go config()), so we override its
49	// ServerConfigCallback to force NoClientAuth=true with a per-username
50	// callback. The library still adds HostKey + PasswordCallback on top of
51	// our config, so password auth keeps working for everyone else.
52	//
53	// Effect: `ssh guest@host -p 2222` and `ssh new@host -p 2222` connect
54	// with no password prompt. `ssh alice@host -p 2222` still prompts.
55	func withReservedNoneAuth() ssh.Option {
56		return func(s *ssh.Server) error {
57			s.ServerConfigCallback = func(ctx ssh.Context) *gossh.ServerConfig {
58				return &gossh.ServerConfig{
59					NoClientAuth: true,
60					NoClientAuthCallback: func(conn gossh.ConnMetadata) (*gossh.Permissions, error) {
61						switch conn.User() {
62						case auth.ReservedUsernameGuest, auth.ReservedUsernameNew:
63							return nil, nil
64						}
65						return nil, errors.New("auth required")
66					},
67				}
68			}
69			return nil
70		}
71	}
72	
73	// makeProgramHandler is the wish ProgramHandler. It owns *tea.Program
74	// creation so we can hand the pointer to the chat broker for live message
75	// delivery, and arrange Unregister when the SSH session ends.
76	func makeProgramHandler(st *store.Store, broker *chat.Broker) bubbletea.ProgramHandler {
77		return func(sess ssh.Session) *tea.Program {
78			ctx := sess.Context()
79	
80			// Register-mode: SSH user was "new". No DB user, no broker registration.
81			// `none` auth bypasses our PasswordHandler, so ctxKeyRegister isn't set
82			// when a client connects via the none-auth path — fall back to inspecting
83			// sess.User() directly.
84			reg, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyRegister).(bool)
85			if reg || sess.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameNew {
86				deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, Broker: broker, IsRegister: true}
87				m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
88				return tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
89			}
90	
91			uid, _ := ctx.Value(ctxKeyUserID).(int64)
92			if uid == 0 {
93				// SSH `none` auth path: NoClientAuthCallback succeeded for
94				// guest but didn't run our PasswordHandler, so user_id was
95				// never stashed. Resolve the guest row here. Any other
96				// username with uid==0 means a misconfiguration — reject.
97				if sess.User() == auth.ReservedUsernameGuest {
98					guest, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(context.Background(), auth.ReservedUsernameGuest)
99					if err == nil && guest.Role == store.RoleGuest {
100						uid = guest.ID
101					}
102				}
103				if uid == 0 {
104					log.Warn("session has no user_id; rejecting", "remote", sess.RemoteAddr(), "user", sess.User())
105					return nil
106				}
107			}
108			user, err := st.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), uid)
109			if err != nil {
110				log.Error("load user", "id", uid, "err", err)
111				return nil
112			}
113	
114			deps := tui.Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: broker}
115			if user.MustChangePassword {
116				deps.MustChangePassword = true
117			}
118			m := tui.NewRoot(deps)
119			p := tea.NewProgram(m, append(bubbletea.MakeOptions(sess), tea.WithAltScreen())...)
120	
121			// Guests are read-only spectators. We deliberately don't register
122			// them in the broker — multiple `ssh guest@...` clients would
123			// collide on the same user.ID, they can't reply to water balloons,
124			// and they don't appear in the online list.
125			if user.Role == store.RoleGuest {
126				log.Info("guest session", "host", deps.User.UserID)
127				return p
128			}
129	
130			// Hook session into the broker so other users can send to us.
131			cs := &chat.Session{UserID: user.ID, UserIDStr: user.UserID, Program: p}
132			broker.Register(cs)
133			log.Info("session registered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
134	
135			// Drain unread water balloons from DB and replay them as toasts.
136			// We do this *before* the program starts so the messages land in
137			// the model's first Update(); tea.Program.Send buffers if needed.
138			go func() {
139				unread, err := st.WaterBalloons().ListUnreadFor(context.Background(), user.ID)
140				if err != nil {
141					log.Warn("drain unread WBs", "user", user.UserID, "err", err)
142					return
143				}
144				for _, w := range unread {
145					p.Send(tui.WBIncomingMsg{ID: w.ID, FromUserID: w.FromUserIDStr, Body: w.Body})
146					_ = st.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), w.ID)
147				}
148			}()
149	
150			// On session end, unregister so we stop trying to deliver to a dead Program.
151			go func() {
152				<-ctx.Done()
153				broker.Unregister(user.ID, p)
154				log.Info("session unregistered", "user", user.UserID, "online", len(broker.OnlineList()))
155			}()
156	
157			return p
158		}
159	}
160
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/users.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type User struct {
11		ID                 int64
12		UserID             string
13		PasswordHash       string
14		Nickname           string
15		RealName           string
16		Email              string
17		NumLogins          int64
18		NumPosts           int64
19		LastLoginAt        sql.NullTime
20		LastHost           sql.NullString
21		CreatedAt          time.Time
22		Role               Role
23		MustChangePassword bool
24	}
25	
26	type UserRepo struct{ s *Store }
27	
28	var (
29		ErrUserNotFound = errors.New("user not found")
30		ErrInvalidRole  = errors.New("invalid role")
31	)
32	
33	const userColumns = `id, user_id, password_hash, nickname, realname, email,
34		num_logins, num_posts, last_login_at, last_host, created_at, role, must_change_password`
35	
36	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
37		u := &User{}
38		var mustChange int64
39		err := row.Scan(
40			&u.ID, &u.UserID, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Nickname, &u.RealName, &u.Email,
41			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
42			&u.Role, &mustChange,
43		)
44		u.MustChangePassword = mustChange != 0
45		return u, err
46	}
47	
48	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
49		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
50		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
51		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
52			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
53			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
54		)
55		if err != nil {
56			return nil, err
57		}
58		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
59		if err != nil {
60			return nil, err
61		}
62		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
63	}
64	
65	func (r *UserRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*User, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		u, err := scanUser(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
70		}
71		return u, err
72	}
73	
74	func (r *UserRepo) GetByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*User, error) {
75		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
76		u, err := scanUser(row)
77		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
78			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
79		}
80		return u, err
81	}
82	
83	func (r *UserRepo) NoteLogin(ctx context.Context, id int64, host string) error {
84		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
85		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
86		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
87			`UPDATE users SET last_login_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, last_host = ?, num_logins = num_logins + 1 WHERE id = ?`,
88			host, id,
89		)
90		return err
91	}
92	
93	func (r *UserRepo) IncrementPosts(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
94		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
95		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
96		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
97			`UPDATE users SET num_posts = num_posts + 1 WHERE id = ?`, id,
98		)
99		return err
100	}
101	
102	// InsertSystemAccount creates a privileged user (admin or guest) with the
103	// given role and must_change_password flag, bypassing the regular Create
104	// path which always defaults role='user'. Used only by the bootstrap seed.
105	func (r *UserRepo) InsertSystemAccount(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash string, role Role, mustChangePassword bool) (*User, error) {
106		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
107		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
108		mc := int64(0)
109		if mustChangePassword {
110			mc = 1
111		}
112		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
113			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, role, must_change_password) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
114			userID, passwordHash, string(role), mc,
115		)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
120		if err != nil {
121			return nil, err
122		}
123		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
124	}
125	
126	// SetPassword updates the password hash and clears must_change_password
127	// in one statement. The TUI password-change screen calls this after
128	// validating the current password and the new-password rules.
129	func (r *UserRepo) SetPassword(ctx context.Context, id int64, passwordHash string) error {
130		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
131		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
132		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
133			`UPDATE users SET password_hash = ?, must_change_password = 0 WHERE id = ?`,
134			passwordHash, id,
135		)
136		return err
137	}
138	
139	// SetRole updates the role of the given user. Callers (admin user-management
140	// screen) are responsible for enforcing higher-level invariants like the
141	// last-admin guard via CountByRole.
142	func (r *UserRepo) SetRole(ctx context.Context, id int64, role Role) error {
143		if !role.Valid() {
144			return ErrInvalidRole
145		}
146		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
147		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
148		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
149			`UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(role), id,
150		)
151		return err
152	}
153	
154	// ListAll returns users ordered by id, paginated. limit<=0 defaults to 20.
155	func (r *UserRepo) ListAll(ctx context.Context, limit, offset int) ([]*User, error) {
156		if limit <= 0 {
157			limit = 20
158		}
159		if offset < 0 {
160			offset = 0
161		}
162		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
163			`SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`,
164			limit, offset,
165		)
166		if err != nil {
167			return nil, err
168		}
169		defer rows.Close()
170		var out []*User
171		for rows.Next() {
172			u, err := scanUser(rows)
173			if err != nil {
174				return nil, err
175			}
176			out = append(out, u)
177		}
178		return out, rows.Err()
179	}
180	
181	// CountByRole returns the number of users currently assigned a given role.
182	// Used by the admin screen's last-admin guard.
183	func (r *UserRepo) CountByRole(ctx context.Context, role Role) (int, error) {
184		var n int
185		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
186			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE role = ?`, string(role),
187		).Scan(&n)
188		return n, err
189	}
190
```

 `./internal/tui/screen_online.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"sort"
6		"strings"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11	)
12	
13	type onlineModel struct {
14		deps    Deps
15		users   []chat.OnlineUser
16		cursor  int
17		width   int
18		height  int
19	}
20	
21	func newOnlineModel(deps Deps) onlineModel {
22		var list []chat.OnlineUser
23		if deps.Broker != nil {
24			list = deps.Broker.OnlineList()
25		}
26		// Stable order: by user_id ascending so the same login is in the same
27		// row each time the screen is opened. The broker's map iteration is
28		// otherwise non-deterministic.
29		sort.Slice(list, func(i, j int) bool { return list[i].UserID < list[j].UserID })
30		return onlineModel{deps: deps, users: list}
31	}
32	
33	func (m onlineModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m onlineModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case tea.KeyMsg:
41			switch msg.String() {
42			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h", "Q":
43				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
44			case "up", "k", "[":
45				if m.cursor > 0 {
46					m.cursor--
47				}
48			case "down", "j", "]":
49				if m.cursor < len(m.users)-1 {
50					m.cursor++
51				}
52			case "home", "g":
53				m.cursor = 0
54			case "end", "G":
55				if len(m.users) > 0 {
56					m.cursor = len(m.users) - 1
57				}
58			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
59				if len(m.users) == 0 {
60					return m, nil
61				}
62				u := m.users[m.cursor]
63				return m, func() tea.Msg {
64					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: u.UserIDStr}
65				}
66			}
67		}
68		return m, nil
69	}
70	
71	func (m onlineModel) View() string {
72		var b strings.Builder
73		b.WriteString("\n")
74		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 線上使用者 Online (%d) ", len(m.users))))
75		b.WriteString("\n\n")
76	
77		if len(m.users) == 0 {
78			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(nobody else online)") + "\n")
79			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Esc/←/h back · Q quit to menu"))
80			return b.String()
81		}
82	
83		const (
84			userW = 14
85			sessW = 10
86		)
87		header := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s",
88			PadRight("UserID", userW),
89			PadRight("Sessions", sessW),
90		)
91		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
92	
93		for i, u := range m.users {
94			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %s  %s",
95				PadRight(u.UserIDStr, userW),
96				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", u.Sessions), sessW),
97			)
98			if i == m.cursor {
99				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
100			} else {
101				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
102			}
103		}
104	
105		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l send water balloon · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
106		b.WriteString("\n")
107		return b.String()
108	}
109
```

```text
1	package tui

3	import (

16	// =====================================================================
17	// Inbox
18	// =====================================================================
19	
20	type mailInboxModel struct {
21		deps    Deps
22		items   []*store.Mail
23		cursor  int
24		width   int
25		height  int
26		loadErr error
27	}
28	
29	func newMailInboxModel(deps Deps) mailInboxModel {
30		items, err := deps.Store.Mail().ListInboxFor(context.Background(), deps.User.ID, 100)
31		return mailInboxModel{deps: deps, items: items, loadErr: err}
32	}
33	
34	func (m mailInboxModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
35	
36	func (m mailInboxModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
37		switch msg := msg.(type) {
38		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
39			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
40			return m, nil
41		case tea.KeyMsg:
42			switch msg.String() {
43			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
44				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
45			case "Q":
46				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
47			case "up", "k", "[":
48				if m.cursor > 0 {
49					m.cursor--
50				}
51			case "down", "j", "]":
52				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
53					m.cursor++
54				}
55			case "c":
56				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailCompose} }
57			case "r":
58				if len(m.items) == 0 {
59					return m, nil
60				}
61				it := m.items[m.cursor]
62				return m, func() tea.Msg {
63					return NavigateMsg{
64						To:        ScreenMailCompose,
65						Recipient: it.FromUserIDStr,
66						MailID:    it.ID,
67					}
68				}
69			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
70				if len(m.items) == 0 {
71					return m, nil
72				}
73				it := m.items[m.cursor]
74				return m, func() tea.Msg {
75					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailThread, MailThreadID: it.ThreadID}
76				}
77			}
78		}
79		return m, nil
80	}
81	
82	func (m mailInboxModel) View() string {
83		var b strings.Builder
84		b.WriteString("\n")
85		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 信箱 Mail "))
86		b.WriteString("\n\n")
87	
88		if m.loadErr != nil {
89			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
90			return b.String()
91		}
92		if len(m.items) == 0 {
93			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no mail — press c to compose)") + "\n")
94			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("c compose · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
95			return b.String()
96		}
97	
98		const (
99			dateW    = 16
100			fromW    = 14
101			statusW  = 5
102		)
103		subjectW := max(20, m.width-2-dateW-1-fromW-1-statusW-3)
104		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",
105			PadRight("Date", dateW),
106			PadRight("From", fromW),
107			PadRight("•", statusW),
108			PadRight("Subject", subjectW),
109		)
110		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
111	
112		for i, it := range m.items {
113			status := "·"
114			if !it.ReadAt.Valid {
115				status = "NEW"
116			}
117			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s",
118				PadRight(it.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"), dateW),
119				PadRight(it.FromUserIDStr, fromW),
120				PadRight(status, statusW),
121				Truncate(it.Subject, subjectW),
122			)
123			if i == m.cursor {
124				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
125			} else {
126				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")

128		}
129	
130		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · r reply · c compose · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
131		b.WriteString("\n")
132		return b.String()
133	}
134	
135	// =====================================================================
136	// Thread view
137	// =====================================================================
138	
139	type mailThreadModel struct {
140		deps     Deps
141		threadID int64
142		items    []*store.Mail
143		scroll   int
144		width    int
145		height   int
146		loadErr  error
147	}
148	
149	func newMailThreadModel(deps Deps, threadID int64) mailThreadModel {
150		ctx := context.Background()
151		items, err := deps.Store.Mail().ListThread(ctx, threadID)
152		if err == nil {
153			// Mark every mail in the thread that's addressed to the current user
154			// as read. A thread you've opened is no longer "new".
155			for _, it := range items {
156				if it.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !it.ReadAt.Valid {
157					_ = deps.Store.Mail().MarkRead(ctx, it.ID)
158				}
159			}
160		}
161		return mailThreadModel{deps: deps, threadID: threadID, items: items, loadErr: err}
162	}
163	
164	func (m mailThreadModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
165	
166	func (m mailThreadModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
167		switch msg := msg.(type) {
168		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
169			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
170			return m, nil
171		case tea.KeyMsg:
172			switch msg.String() {
173			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
174				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailInbox} }
175			case "Q":
176				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
177			case "up", "k":
178				if m.scroll > 0 {
179					m.scroll--
180				}
181			case "down", "j":
182				m.scroll++
183			case "g":
184				m.scroll = 0
185			case "r":
186				// Reply to the LAST message in the thread (whoever sent it).
187				if len(m.items) == 0 {
188					return m, nil
189				}
190				last := m.items[len(m.items)-1]
191				recipient := last.FromUserIDStr
192				if last.FromUserID == m.deps.User.ID && last.ParentID.Valid {
193					// We sent the last one; reply to the user we were replying to.
194					if parent, err := m.deps.Store.Mail().GetByID(context.Background(), last.ParentID.Int64); err == nil {
195						recipient = parent.FromUserIDStr
196					}
197				}
198				return m, func() tea.Msg {
199					return NavigateMsg{
200						To:        ScreenMailCompose,
201						Recipient: recipient,
202						MailID:    last.ID,
203					}
204				}
205			}
206		}
207		return m, nil
208	}
209	
210	func (m mailThreadModel) View() string {
211		var b strings.Builder
212		b.WriteString("\n")
213		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 信件 Thread #%d ", m.threadID)))
214		b.WriteString("\n\n")
215	
216		if m.loadErr != nil {
217			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
218			return b.String()
219		}
220		if len(m.items) == 0 {
221			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(empty thread)") + "\n")
222			return b.String()
223		}
224	
225		for i, it := range m.items {
226			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── #%d  %s → %s  %s ──",
227				i+1,
228				it.FromUserIDStr,
229				recipientName(m.deps, it.ToUserID),
230				it.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
231			)) + "\n")
232			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Subject: ") + it.Subject + "\n")
233			b.WriteString("  " + indent(it.Body, "  ") + "\n\n")
234		}
235	
236		b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("r reply · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
237		b.WriteString("\n")
238		return b.String()
239	}
240	
241	// recipientName looks up the display handle for a user_id; falls back to
242	// the numeric id if the lookup fails (best-effort, View() must not error).
243	func recipientName(deps Deps, userID int64) string {
244		if deps.Store == nil {
245			return fmt.Sprintf("uid=%d", userID)
246		}
247		u, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), userID)
248		if err != nil || u == nil {
249			return fmt.Sprintf("uid=%d", userID)
250		}
251		return u.UserID
252	}
253	
254	// =====================================================================
255	// Compose
256	// =====================================================================
257	
258	const (
259		mailFocusTo = iota
260		mailFocusSubject
261		mailFocusBody
262	)
263	
264	type mailComposeModel struct {
265		deps     Deps
266		parentID int64 // 0 ⇒ new thread; else reply
267		to       textinput.Model
268		subject  textinput.Model
269		body     textarea.Model
270		focus    int
271		width    int
272		height   int
273		err      string
274		sent     string // success: recipient userid
275	}
276	
277	func newMailComposeModel(deps Deps, recipient string, parentID int64) mailComposeModel {
278		to := textinput.New()
279		to.Placeholder = "alice"
280		to.CharLimit = 12
281		to.Width = 30
282		to.SetValue(recipient)
283	
284		subj := textinput.New()
285		subj.Placeholder = "subject"
286		subj.CharLimit = 64
287		subj.Width = 60
288	
289		body := textarea.New()
290		body.Placeholder = "message body…"
291		body.CharLimit = 4000
292		body.SetWidth(60)
293		body.SetHeight(8)
294	
295		// On reply, default the subject from the parent (with "Re: " prefix
296		// once and only once).
297		if parentID != 0 && deps.Store != nil {
298			if parent, err := deps.Store.Mail().GetByID(context.Background(), parentID); err == nil {
299				s := parent.Subject
300				if !strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(s), "re:") {
301					s = "Re: " + s
302				}
303				subj.SetValue(s)
304			}
305		}
306	
307		m := mailComposeModel{
308			deps:     deps,
309			parentID: parentID,
310			to:       to,
311			subject:  subj,
312			body:     body,
313		}
314		switch {
315		case recipient == "":
316			m.focus = mailFocusTo
317			m.to.Focus()
318		case subj.Value() == "":
319			m.focus = mailFocusSubject
320			m.subject.Focus()
321		default:
322			m.focus = mailFocusBody
323			m.body.Focus()
324		}
325		return m
326	}
327	
328	func (m mailComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
329	
330	func (m mailComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
331		switch msg := msg.(type) {
332		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
333			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
334			m.body.SetWidth(max(40, msg.Width-12))
335			return m, nil
336	
337		case tea.KeyMsg:
338			if m.sent != "" {
339				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailInbox} }
340			}
341			switch msg.String() {
342			case "esc":
343				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailInbox} }
344			case "tab":
345				m.cycleFocus()
346				return m, nil
347			case "ctrl+s":
348				return m.submit()
349			}
350	
351			switch m.focus {
352			case mailFocusTo:
353				if k := msg.String(); k == "enter" {
354					m.cycleFocus()
355					return m, nil
356				}
357				var cmd tea.Cmd
358				m.to, cmd = m.to.Update(msg)
359				return m, cmd
360			case mailFocusSubject:
361				if k := msg.String(); k == "enter" {
362					m.cycleFocus()
363					return m, nil
364				}
365				var cmd tea.Cmd
366				m.subject, cmd = m.subject.Update(msg)
367				return m, cmd
368			}
369			var cmd tea.Cmd
370			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
371			return m, cmd
372		}
373		return m, nil
374	}
375	
376	func (m *mailComposeModel) cycleFocus() {
377		m.to.Blur()
378		m.subject.Blur()
379		m.body.Blur()
380		m.focus = (m.focus + 1) % 3
381		switch m.focus {
382		case mailFocusTo:
383			m.to.Focus()
384		case mailFocusSubject:
385			m.subject.Focus()
386		case mailFocusBody:
387			m.body.Focus()
388		}
389	}
390	
391	func (m mailComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
392		to := strings.TrimSpace(m.to.Value())
393		subj := strings.TrimSpace(m.subject.Value())
394		body := strings.TrimSpace(m.body.Value())
395		if to == "" {
396			m.err = "recipient required"
397			return m, nil
398		}
399		if subj == "" {
400			m.err = "subject required"
401			return m, nil
402		}
403		if body == "" {
404			m.err = "body required"
405			return m, nil
406		}
407	
408		ctx := context.Background()
409		target, err := m.deps.Store.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, to)
410		if err != nil {
411			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
412				m.err = "no such user: " + to
413			} else {
414				m.err = err.Error()
415			}
416			return m, nil
417		}
418		from := m.deps.User
419		var parentPtr *int64
420		if m.parentID != 0 {
421			p := m.parentID
422			parentPtr = &p
423		}
424		mail, err := m.deps.Store.Mail().Insert(ctx, from.ID, from.UserID, target.ID, subj, body, parentPtr)
425		if err != nil {
426			m.err = err.Error()
427			return m, nil
428		}
429		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
430			// Notify the recipient so an open inbox can refresh. Distinct from
431			// water balloons (which always toast); mail is "silent" delivery.
432			m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, MailIncomingMsg{
433				ID:         mail.ID,
434				ThreadID:   mail.ThreadID,
435				FromUserID: from.UserID,
436				Subject:    subj,
437			})
438		}
439		m.sent = target.UserID
440		return m, nil
441	}
442	
443	func (m mailComposeModel) View() string {
444		var b strings.Builder
445		b.WriteString("\n")
446		if m.parentID == 0 {
447			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 寫信 New Mail "))
448		} else {
449			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 回信 Reply "))
450		}
451		b.WriteString("\n\n")
452	
453		if m.sent != "" {
454			b.WriteString("  " + StyleSuccess.Render("✓ sent to "+m.sent) + "\n\n")
455			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("any key returns to inbox"))
456			return b.String()
457		}
458	
459		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("To (userid):") + "\n")
460		b.WriteString("  " + m.to.View() + "\n\n")
461		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Subject:") + "\n")
462		b.WriteString("  " + m.subject.View() + "\n\n")
463		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
464		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
465	
466		if m.err != "" {
467			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
468		}
469		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab cycle field · Enter (in To/Subject) → next · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
470		return b.String()
471	}
472
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/store.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"fmt"
7		"sync"
8	
9		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
10	)
11	
12	// Store wraps the *sql.DB plus a process-level write mutex used for
13	// multi-statement writes that need to be atomic at the application level
14	// (e.g. inserting a push and recomputing the cached recommend_score).
15	// SQLite WAL handles concurrent reads; this mutex avoids SQLITE_BUSY under
16	// modest contention without spreading sql.Tx everywhere.
17	type Store struct {
18		db      *sql.DB
19		writeMu sync.Mutex
20	
21		users          *UserRepo
22		boards         *BoardRepo
23		articles       *ArticleRepo
24		pushes         *PushRepo
25		waterBalloons  *WaterBalloonRepo
26		mail           *MailRepo
27	}
28	
29	func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
30		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
31			"file:%s?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(on)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)",
32			path,
33		)
34		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
37		}
38		if err := db.PingContext(context.Background()); err != nil {
39			_ = db.Close()
40			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping sqlite: %w", err)
41		}
42		if err := apply(context.Background(), db); err != nil {
43			_ = db.Close()
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: %w", err)
45		}
46	
47		s := &Store{db: db}
48		s.users = &UserRepo{s: s}
49		s.boards = &BoardRepo{s: s}
50		s.articles = &ArticleRepo{s: s}
51		s.pushes = &PushRepo{s: s}
52		s.waterBalloons = &WaterBalloonRepo{s: s}
53		s.mail = &MailRepo{s: s}
54		return s, nil
55	}
56	
57	func (s *Store) Close() error { return s.db.Close() }
58	
59	// DB exposes the raw handle for callers that need it (tests, ad-hoc SQL).
60	// Production code should go through repos.
61	func (s *Store) DB() *sql.DB { return s.db }
62	
63	func (s *Store) Users() *UserRepo                 { return s.users }
64	func (s *Store) Boards() *BoardRepo               { return s.boards }
65	func (s *Store) Articles() *ArticleRepo           { return s.articles }
66	func (s *Store) Pushes() *PushRepo                { return s.pushes }
67	func (s *Store) WaterBalloons() *WaterBalloonRepo { return s.waterBalloons }
68	func (s *Store) Mail() *MailRepo                  { return s.mail }
69
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	// Package storetest provides shared helpers for tests that need a fresh
2	// SQLite store. It lives in its own package so any internal package can
3	// import it without creating cycles.
4	package storetest
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// New opens a fresh on-disk SQLite store in t.TempDir(), runs migrations,
15	// and registers a Cleanup to close it. Use it whenever you'd write
16	// `store.Open` in a test — never share stores across tests.
17	func New(tb testing.TB) *store.Store {
18		tb.Helper()
19		dir := tb.TempDir()
20		st, err := store.Open(filepath.Join(dir, "test.db"))
21		if err != nil {
22			tb.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
23		}
24		tb.Cleanup(func() { _ = st.Close() })
25		return st
26	}
27	
28	// MustUser inserts a user with a placeholder password hash and returns it.
29	// Convenient for tests that need a logged-in identity but don't care about auth.
30	func MustUser(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, userID, nickname string) *store.User {
31		tb.Helper()
32		u, err := st.Users().Create(context.Background(), userID, "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.", nickname, "")
33		if err != nil {
34			tb.Fatalf("create user %q: %v", userID, err)
35		}
36		return u
37	}
38	
39	// MustBoard returns a seeded board by name, failing the test if not found.
40	// Useful with the default Welcome / Test / ChitChat boards which
41	// SeedDefaults / migration 0002 create automatically.
42	func MustBoard(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, name string) *store.Board {
43		tb.Helper()
44		b, err := st.Boards().GetByName(context.Background(), name)
45		if err != nil {
46			tb.Fatalf("get board %q: %v", name, err)
47		}
48		return b
49	}
50
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/mail.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Mail struct {
11		ID            int64
12		ThreadID      int64
13		ParentID      sql.NullInt64
14		FromUserID    int64
15		FromUserIDStr string
16		ToUserID      int64
17		Subject       string
18		Body          string
19		ReadAt        sql.NullTime
20		CreatedAt     time.Time
21	}
22	
23	type MailRepo struct{ s *Store }
24	
25	var ErrMailNotFound = errors.New("mail not found")
26	
27	const mailColumns = `id, thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body, read_at, created_at`
28	
29	func scanMail(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Mail, error) {
30		m := &Mail{}
31		if err := row.Scan(
32			&m.ID, &m.ThreadID, &m.ParentID, &m.FromUserID, &m.FromUserIDStr,
33			&m.ToUserID, &m.Subject, &m.Body, &m.ReadAt, &m.CreatedAt,
34		); err != nil {
35			return nil, err
36		}
37		return m, nil
38	}
39	
40	// Insert creates a new mail. If parentID is nil, this is a thread root and
41	// thread_id is back-filled to the new row's own id under writeMu so the
42	// invariant `thread_id IS NOT NULL` holds for every read.
43	func (r *MailRepo) Insert(ctx context.Context, fromID int64, fromUserID string, toID int64, subject, body string, parentID *int64) (*Mail, error) {
44		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
45		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
46	
47		var threadID sql.NullInt64
48		var parent sql.NullInt64
49		if parentID != nil {
50			parent = sql.NullInt64{Int64: *parentID, Valid: true}
51			// Look up the parent's thread_id so the new reply joins the same thread.
52			row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT thread_id FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, *parentID)
53			var t sql.NullInt64
54			if err := row.Scan(&t); err != nil {
55				if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
56					return nil, ErrMailNotFound
57				}
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			threadID = t
61		}
62	
63		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
64			`INSERT INTO mail (thread_id, parent_id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, subject, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
65			threadID, parent, fromID, fromUserID, toID, subject, body,
66		)
67		if err != nil {
68			return nil, err
69		}
70		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
71		if err != nil {
72			return nil, err
73		}
74		if !threadID.Valid {
75			// Root mail — thread_id = id.
76			if _, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `UPDATE mail SET thread_id = ? WHERE id = ?`, id, id); err != nil {
77				return nil, err
78			}
79		}
80		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, id)
81		return scanMail(row)
82	}
83	
84	// ListInboxFor returns the recipient's inbox: unread first, then read,
85	// each group ordered newest-first. Mirrors WaterBalloonRepo.ListInboxFor.
86	func (r *MailRepo) ListInboxFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64, limit int) ([]*Mail, error) {
87		if limit <= 0 {
88			limit = 50
89		}
90		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
91			`SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE to_user_id = ?
92			 ORDER BY (read_at IS NULL) DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
93			toUserID, limit,
94		)
95		if err != nil {
96			return nil, err
97		}
98		defer rows.Close()
99		var out []*Mail
100		for rows.Next() {
101			m, err := scanMail(rows)
102			if err != nil {
103				return nil, err
104			}
105			out = append(out, m)
106		}
107		return out, rows.Err()
108	}
109	
110	// ListThread returns every mail in a thread, oldest first (chronological
111	// reading order — matches how the article-view-style thread screen reads).
112	func (r *MailRepo) ListThread(ctx context.Context, threadID int64) ([]*Mail, error) {
113		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
114			`SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE thread_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
115			threadID,
116		)
117		if err != nil {
118			return nil, err
119		}
120		defer rows.Close()
121		var out []*Mail
122		for rows.Next() {
123			m, err := scanMail(rows)
124			if err != nil {
125				return nil, err
126			}
127			out = append(out, m)
128		}
129		return out, rows.Err()
130	}
131	
132	func (r *MailRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Mail, error) {
133		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+mailColumns+` FROM mail WHERE id = ?`, id)
134		m, err := scanMail(row)
135		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
136			return nil, ErrMailNotFound
137		}
138		return m, err
139	}
140	
141	func (r *MailRepo) MarkRead(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
142		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
143		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
144		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
145			`UPDATE mail SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, id,
146		)
147		return err
148	}
149	
150	func (r *MailRepo) MarkAllReadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) error {
151		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
152		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
153		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
154			`UPDATE mail SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`,
155			toUserID,
156		)
157		return err
158	}
159	
160	// CountUnreadFor returns the number of unread mails for the recipient,
161	// useful for the main-menu badge.
162	func (r *MailRepo) CountUnreadFor(ctx context.Context, toUserID int64) (int, error) {
163		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
164			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mail WHERE to_user_id = ? AND read_at IS NULL`, toUserID,
165		)
166		var n int
167		if err := row.Scan(&n); err != nil {
168			return 0, err
169		}
170		return n, nil
171	}
172
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
```
```text
4		"testing"
```

```text
6		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
9	)
10	
11	// keyOf builds a tea.KeyMsg whose .String() matches the wanted form.
12	// bubbletea exposes typed key constants; we use Runes for ASCII letters
13	// and named Type values for special keys.
14	func keyOf(s string) tea.KeyMsg {
15		switch s {
16		case "enter":
17			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEnter}
18		case "esc":
19			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyEsc}
20		case "left":
21			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyLeft}
22		case "right":
23			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRight}
24		case "up":
25			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyUp}
26		case "down":
27			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown}
28		case "backspace":
29			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyBackspace}
30		case "ctrl+u":
31			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlU}
32		case "ctrl+c":
33			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyCtrlC}
34		case " ":
35			return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeySpace}
36		}
37		return tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune(s)}
38	}
39	
40	// runCmd invokes the tea.Cmd returned by Update and returns the resulting
41	// tea.Msg (or nil if cmd is nil).
42	func runCmd(cmd tea.Cmd) tea.Msg {
43		if cmd == nil {
44			return nil
45		}
46		return cmd()
47	}
48	
49	func newMainMenuFixture() (mainMenuModel, Deps) {
50		deps := Deps{
51			User: &store.User{ID: 1, UserID: "alice", Nickname: "Alice"},
52		}
53		return newMainMenuModel(deps), deps

59			t.Fatalf("initial cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
60		}
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"regexp"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode/utf8"
7	
8		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
9	)
10	
11	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
12	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
13	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
14	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
15	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
16		if w <= 0 {
17			return ""
18		}
19		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
20		if cw == w {
21			return s
22		}
23		if cw > w {
24			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
25		}
26		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
27	}
28	
29	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
30	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
31		if w <= 0 {
32			return ""
33		}
34		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
35		if cw == w {
36			return s
37		}
38		if cw > w {
39			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
40		}
41		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
42	}
43	
44	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
45	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
46		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
47			return s
48		}
49		if w <= 1 {
50			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
51		}
52		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
53	}
54	
55	// Width returns the display width of s.
56	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
57	
58	// ansiCSIRe matches a CSI escape sequence: ESC '[' optional-params letter.
59	// SGR (color), cursor moves, and most other terminal control codes used by
60	// banners follow this shape. Other escape kinds (OSC, DCS) are left
61	// untouched — banners almost never carry them.
62	var ansiCSIRe = regexp.MustCompile("\x1b\\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]")
63	
64	// stripCSI returns s with CSI escape sequences removed. Use it for any
65	// width / column calculation that involves banner content (or any other
66	// string that may carry inline color codes). The four helpers above
67	// intentionally do NOT call this — they're the article-list table path
68	// where input is plain text, and stripping every line would just be
69	// overhead.
70	func stripCSI(s string) string {
71		return ansiCSIRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
72	}
73	
74	// bannerVisualWidth returns the display width of s with CSI escapes
75	// excluded from the count.
76	func bannerVisualWidth(s string) int {
77		return runewidth.StringWidth(stripCSI(s))
78	}
79	
80	// bannerTruncate clips s to display-width w, preserving inline CSI runs
81	// (color codes are zero-width). On overflow the result ends with "…"
82	// (which counts toward the budget). If the input contained any CSI, the
83	// result is suffixed with `\x1b[0m` so a forgotten reset doesn't bleed
84	// into subsequent terminal output.
85	func bannerTruncate(s string, w int) string {
86		if w <= 0 {
87			return ""
88		}
89		if bannerVisualWidth(s) <= w {
90			return ensureANSIReset(s)
91		}
92		budget := w - 1 // reserve one cell for "…"
93		var out strings.Builder
94		visible := 0
95		cursor := 0
96		for _, loc := range ansiCSIRe.FindAllStringIndex(s, -1) {
97			if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:loc[0]], &visible, budget) {
98				out.WriteString("…")
99				return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
100			}
101			out.WriteString(s[loc[0]:loc[1]])
102			cursor = loc[1]
103		}
104		if !writeBodyUpToBudget(&out, s[cursor:], &visible, budget) {
105			out.WriteString("…")
106			return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
107		}
108		out.WriteString("…")
109		return ensureANSIReset(out.String())
110	}
111	
112	func writeBodyUpToBudget(out *strings.Builder, body string, visible *int, budget int) bool {
113		for i := 0; i < len(body); {
114			r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(body[i:])
115			rw := runewidth.RuneWidth(r)
116			if *visible+rw > budget {
117				return false
118			}
119			out.WriteRune(r)
120			*visible += rw
121			i += size
122		}
123		return true
124	}
125	
126	func ensureANSIReset(s string) string {
127		if strings.Contains(s, "\x1b[") && !strings.HasSuffix(s, "\x1b[0m") {
128			return s + "\x1b[0m"
129		}
130		return s
131	}
132
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
48		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
49			switch k.String() {
50			case "q", "esc":

52			case "up", "k":

55				}

59					m.cursor++
60				}
61				return m, nil
62			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":

64				if it.to == -1 {
65					return m, tea.Quit
66				}

68			case "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6":

74					}
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrate.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"embed"
7		"fmt"
8		"sort"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11	)
12	
13	//go:embed migrations/*.sql
14	var migrationsFS embed.FS
15	
16	// apply runs every migration whose version number isn't already in
17	// schema_migrations. Filenames must be NNNN_name.sql; the leading integer is
18	// the version. Migrations are applied in ascending version order, each in
19	// its own transaction.
20	func apply(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {
21		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, `
22			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
23				version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
24				applied_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
25			)
26		`); err != nil {
27			return fmt.Errorf("create schema_migrations: %w", err)
28		}
29	
30		applied, err := loadApplied(ctx, db)
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34	
35		entries, err := migrationsFS.ReadDir("migrations")
36		if err != nil {
37			return fmt.Errorf("read embedded migrations: %w", err)
38		}
39		type migration struct {
40			version  int
41			filename string
42		}
43		var pending []migration
44		for _, e := range entries {
45			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".sql") {
46				continue
47			}
48			v, err := parseVersion(e.Name())
49			if err != nil {
50				return err
51			}
52			if !applied[v] {
53				pending = append(pending, migration{version: v, filename: e.Name()})
54			}
55		}
56		sort.Slice(pending, func(i, j int) bool { return pending[i].version < pending[j].version })
57	
58		for _, m := range pending {
59			body, err := migrationsFS.ReadFile("migrations/" + m.filename)
60			if err != nil {
61				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", m.filename, err)
62			}
63			if err := runOne(ctx, db, m.version, string(body)); err != nil {
64				return fmt.Errorf("apply %s: %w", m.filename, err)
65			}
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	func loadApplied(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (map[int]bool, error) {
71		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, `SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("load applied migrations: %w", err)
74		}
75		defer rows.Close()
76		out := map[int]bool{}
77		for rows.Next() {
78			var v int
79			if err := rows.Scan(&v); err != nil {
80				return nil, err
81			}
82			out[v] = true
83		}
84		return out, rows.Err()
85	}
86	
87	func runOne(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, version int, body string) error {
88		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
89		if err != nil {
90			return err
91		}
92		defer tx.Rollback()
93		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, body); err != nil {
94			return err
95		}
96		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations(version) VALUES (?)`, version); err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		return tx.Commit()
100	}
101	
102	func parseVersion(filename string) (int, error) {
103		// "0001_init.sql" -> 1
104		idx := strings.IndexByte(filename, '_')
105		if idx <= 0 {
106			return 0, fmt.Errorf("migration filename missing version prefix: %s", filename)
107		}
108		v, err := strconv.Atoi(filename[:idx])
109		if err != nil {
110			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse version in %s: %w", filename, err)
111		}
112		return v, nil
113	}
114
```
</details></tool-use>

Self-target is not blocked. I have everything needed. Now I'll deliver the plan as my final assistant message.

---

# Phase 1 Implementation Plan: 水球 Conversation Threads

A conversation-thread layer over the existing `water_balloons` table — no schema migration. Inbox switches from a flat list to a per-counterparty roll-up; Enter drills into a chronological two-direction transcript with that user; read-marking moves from "open inbox" to "open this thread."

## 1. Repo additions: `internal/store/waterballoons.go`

Three new methods. No new index needed for Phase 1 (BBS-scale data: per-recipient row counts are small, and the existing `idx_wb_to_unread (to_user_id, read_at)` already covers `MarkConversationRead`'s `WHERE`. The two-way `ListConversation` query plans as two index seeks via SQLite's OR-to-UNION rewrite — fine until per-pair history grows to the thousands; revisit if we ever build "load older").

### 1a. Counterparty struct (new)

```go
// WBCounterparty is one row of the per-counterparty inbox roll-up. ID/UserID
// are the OTHER user (not the viewer).
type WBCounterparty struct {
    UserID      int64
    UserIDStr   string
    LastBody    string
    LastFromMe  bool
    LastAt      time.Time
    UnreadCount int64 // unread *to* the viewer from this counterparty
}
```

`LastBody` / `LastFromMe` are nice-to-have for the inbox preview row ("you: …" vs "alice: …"). Add now since it costs one more column in the SELECT and matches Mail-inbox conventions.

### 1b. `ListCounterpartiesFor(ctx, userID, limit) ([]*WBCounterparty, error)`

The trickiest piece. The "derived counterparty id" (`CASE WHEN from_user_id = ? THEN to_user_id ELSE from_user_id END`) is computed once in a CTE so we can `GROUP BY` it cheaply. Then we LEFT JOIN that aggregate against the latest message row per counterparty to get `LastBody` / `LastFromMe`.

```sql
WITH related AS (
    SELECT
        id,
        from_user_id,
        from_userid,
        to_user_id,
        body,
        read_at,
        created_at,
        CASE WHEN from_user_id = ?1 THEN to_user_id ELSE from_user_id END AS cp_id
    FROM water_balloons
    WHERE from_user_id = ?1 OR to_user_id = ?1
),
agg AS (
    SELECT
        cp_id,
        MAX(id)   AS last_id,
        MAX(created_at) AS last_at,
        SUM(CASE WHEN to_user_id = ?1 AND read_at IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unread_count
    FROM related
    GROUP BY cp_id
)
SELECT
    a.cp_id,
    u.user_id     AS cp_userid_str,
    r.body        AS last_body,
    (r.from_user_id = ?1) AS last_from_me,
    a.last_at,
    a.unread_count
FROM agg a
JOIN related r ON r.id = a.last_id
JOIN users    u ON u.id = a.cp_id
ORDER BY (a.unread_count > 0) DESC, a.last_at DESC
LIMIT ?2;
```

Notes:
- `MAX(id)` not `MAX(created_at)` for last-row resolution; `id` is monotonic, `created_at` resolves to seconds in SQLite and can tie. `last_at` for display is still `MAX(created_at)` so the column reads naturally.
- The outer `ORDER BY (unread_count > 0) DESC, last_at DESC` puts conversations with unread first (matches `ListInboxFor`'s "unread first" convention from `waterballoons.go:86`), tie-broken by recency.
- Joining `users` makes the result tolerant of a renamed counterparty: we always show their *current* `user_id` (handle), not whatever was denormalized into `from_userid` on insert. See concern #3 below.
- `?1` and `?2` use sqlite numbered params — SQLite accepts both `?` and `?N`; modernc/sqlite supports it. If you want strict positional, repeat `?` and pass `userID` three times then `limit`.

### 1c. `ListConversation(ctx, viewerID, counterpartyID, limit) ([]*WaterBalloon, error)`

```sql
SELECT id, from_user_id, from_userid, to_user_id, body, delivered_live, read_at, created_at
FROM water_balloons
WHERE (from_user_id = ? AND to_user_id = ?)
   OR (from_user_id = ? AND to_user_id = ?)
ORDER BY id ASC
LIMIT ?
```

Args: `(viewerID, counterpartyID, counterpartyID, viewerID, limit)`. ASC order so the newest is at the bottom (chat-style scrollback). `LIMIT 200` default. Phase 1 doesn't paginate; if a conversation exceeds the limit we silently truncate the oldest. That's acceptable for now — note in a TODO comment.

### 1d. `MarkConversationRead(ctx, viewerID, counterpartyID) error`

```sql
UPDATE water_balloons
SET read_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
WHERE to_user_id = ?
  AND from_user_id = ?
  AND read_at IS NULL
```

Holds `s.writeMu` like the other mutators (`waterballoons.go:104`). Idempotent by design — second call no-ops because `read_at IS NULL` filter is empty.

## 2. Inbox screen rework: `internal/tui/screen_wb.go`

Change `wbInboxModel` from a flat-message list to a counterparty list.

### 2a. Constructor changes (`screen_wb.go:29-37`)

- Replace items field type: `items []*store.WaterBalloon` → `items []*store.WBCounterparty`.
- Call `ListCounterpartiesFor` instead of `ListInboxFor`.
- **Delete the `MarkAllReadFor` call at line 34** (the entire `if err == nil {…}` block). Read-marking now happens in the thread screen.

### 2b. Update changes (`screen_wb.go:41-71`)

- `r`/`enter`/`space`/`right`/`l` now navigates to `ScreenWBThread` (not `ScreenWBCompose`), passing the counterparty:
  ```go
  it := m.items[m.cursor]
  return m, func() tea.Msg {
      return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: it.UserID}
  }
  ```
- `c` continues to open `ScreenWBCompose` with no recipient (compose-anyone path).
- Optional but cheap: add `Ctrl+R` "refresh" so a user who lingers on the inbox can pull new counterparty rows after a `WBIncomingMsg`. Defer auto-refresh on `WBIncomingMsg` to a separate Update case (see §4).

### 2c. View changes (`screen_wb.go:73-124`)

Columns: Date · From · Unread · Last message preview ("you: hi" / "alice: hi"). Keep `PadRight`/`Truncate` and the existing column-width math (`screen_wb.go:89-100`). Replace `it.FromUserIDStr` → `it.UserIDStr`, `it.Body` → preview, `status := "NEW" / "·"` → `unreadCount` printed when > 0:

```go
status := "·"
if it.UnreadCount > 0 {
    status = fmt.Sprintf("%d", it.UnreadCount)
}
preview := it.LastBody
if it.LastFromMe {
    preview = "you: " + preview
}
```

Help line: `↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · c compose · Esc/←/h back`.

## 3. New thread screen: `wbThreadModel`

Keep the file lean — add to the bottom of `screen_wb.go` (mirrors how compose lives there). The inbox + compose + thread are tightly coupled and ~200 lines combined; one file is more readable than three.

### 3a. Constructor

```go
type wbThreadModel struct {
    deps     Deps
    cpID     int64           // counterparty user.ID
    cpUserID string          // counterparty handle (for header + reply path)
    items    []*store.WaterBalloon
    scroll   int             // top-line offset; clamped in View
    width    int
    height   int
    loadErr  error
}

func newWBThreadModel(deps Deps, counterpartyID int64) wbThreadModel {
    ctx := context.Background()
    cp, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, counterpartyID)
    if err != nil {
        return wbThreadModel{deps: deps, cpID: counterpartyID, loadErr: err}
    }
    items, err := deps.Store.WaterBalloons().ListConversation(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID, 200)
    if err == nil {
        // Mark only the inbound side as read. This is the new mark-read trigger.
        _ = deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkConversationRead(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID)
        // Reflect the mark in our local copy so View doesn't render stale "NEW".
        now := time.Now()
        for _, w := range items {
            if w.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !w.ReadAt.Valid {
                w.ReadAt = sql.NullTime{Time: now, Valid: true}
            }
        }
    }
    return wbThreadModel{
        deps:    deps,
        cpID:    counterpartyID,
        cpUserID: cp.UserID,
        items:   items,
        loadErr: err,
    }
}
```

Mirrors `mailThreadModel`'s mark-read-on-open pattern (`screen_mail.go:149-162`).

### 3b. Update — keys

```go
case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
    return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
case "Q":
    return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
case "up", "k":
    if m.scroll > 0 { m.scroll-- }
case "down", "j":
    m.scroll++ // clamped at render time
case "g", "home":
    m.scroll = 0
case "G", "end":
    m.scroll = m.maxScroll() // helper using len(items) and visible height
case "c", "r":
    return m, func() tea.Msg {
        return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: m.cpUserID}
    }
```

`c` and `r` both go to compose pre-filled. `r` because muscle memory from the inbox; `c` for symmetry with the rest of the app. **Phase 2 will replace this** with an inline textinput, but the keymap is forward-compatible: Phase 2 keeps the same keys, they just toggle focus rather than navigate away.

### 3c. Update — live append (recommendation: include in Phase 1)

**Recommendation: include the minimum hook now.** It's load-bearing for Phase 2, and it's small (~15 lines). It also fixes a subtle UX bug: today, if alice opens her thread with bob and bob sends a WB while alice has the thread open, alice sees the toast (good) but the thread doesn't update (bad — she has to back out and re-enter).

```go
case WBIncomingMsg:
    if !m.matchesCounterparty(msg.FromUserID) {
        return m, nil
    }
    ctx := context.Background()
    if wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().GetByID(ctx, msg.ID); err == nil {
        m.items = append(m.items, wb)
    }
    // Mark read since it's now visible on screen.
    _ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, msg.ID)
    return m, nil
```

This requires:
- A new `WaterBalloonRepo.GetByID(ctx, id) (*WaterBalloon, error)` (one-liner using `wbColumns`). Add it to §1 alongside the others.
- A `(m wbThreadModel) matchesCounterparty(handle string) bool` that compares to `m.cpUserID` case-insensitively. (`equalFold` from `chat/broker.go:142` could move up to a shared util — or just inline `strings.EqualFold`.)

**Toast suppression deferred to Phase 2.** Today's behavior — toast fires, thread also appends — is fine for Phase 1; the user gets visible confirmation. Phase 2 will add a "is the active screen a matching thread?" check in `Root.Update` to suppress duplicate toasts.

### 3d. View

Chat-style scrollback. Each item:

```
  alice  2026-05-06 14:23
    hi how's it going
  
  you    2026-05-06 14:24
    pretty good thanks
```

Sender label uses `StyleHighlight` for messages from the counterparty (incoming) and `StyleDim` for outgoing. Wrap body to width-4 (or so), one message per "card". Use a viewport-style render: skip the first `m.scroll` lines, render up to `m.height-4` lines after that.

## 4. Routing: `internal/tui/messages.go` and `root.go`

### 4a. New screen constant

In `messages.go:6-24` (after `ScreenWBCompose` and before `ScreenOnline` to keep WB screens contiguous):

```go
ScreenWBThread
```

### 4b. NavigateMsg field — recommendation: add a new field

**Recommendation: add `CounterpartyUserID int64` rather than reuse `Recipient`.**

`Recipient` is documented (`messages.go:32`) as "prefilled recipient userid" — a *string handle* used by compose screens. The thread screen needs the *numeric ID* for the SQL queries. Reusing `Recipient` would force every navigate site to do a `Users().GetByUserID` lookup just to pass it back. Adding a field is one line.

```go
CounterpartyUserID int64 // for ScreenWBThread: which DM thread to open
```

(Yes, `MailThreadID` is also `int64` and feels parallel, but it points to a `mail.thread_id`, a different concept. Don't conflate.)

### 4c. New navigate case in `root.go:151-189`

```go
case ScreenWBThread:
    sub = newWBThreadModel(m.deps, n.CounterpartyUserID)
```

Place it next to `ScreenWBInbox` / `ScreenWBCompose` for grouping.

## 5. Bonus: online-list entry point (Phase 1, optional)

The user mentioned this as Phase 2 territory ("we may want to also offer 'open thread with X'"). Cheapest approach: add a new key to `screen_online.go` Update — say `t` — that opens the thread instead of compose. Leave Enter alone (current behavior: opens compose).

```go
case "t":
    if len(m.users) == 0 { return m, nil }
    u := m.users[m.cursor]
    return m, func() tea.Msg {
        return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: u.UserID}
    }
```

This is genuinely 6 lines of code — recommend including in Phase 1. Update the help-line in `screen_online.go:105` to mention `t`.

## 6. Tests

### 6a. Repo: `internal/store/waterballoons_test.go`

- `TestWaterBalloons_ListConversation_BothDirections` — alice→bob and bob→alice rows interleaved; assert results contain both, ASC by id, length matches.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListConversation_FiltersOtherUsers` — alice, bob, carol; insert wb between every pair; query (alice, bob) and assert no carol rows leak.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListConversation_Limit` — insert 5, request limit=3, expect 3 oldest? newest? **Decision: oldest 3** (current SQL says `ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 3`). Document this — for chat-history "show more older" we'd want newest with descending, but Phase 1 ignores pagination. Add a TODO in the SQL comment.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_Grouping` — alice has 3 with bob, 2 with carol; expect 2 rows, in `LastAt DESC` order.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_UnreadCount` — alice receives 4 from bob (none read), 1 from carol (read); assert `UnreadCount==4` for bob row, `==0` for carol.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_LastFromMe` — last message in (alice, bob) was sent BY alice; verify `LastFromMe==true` and `LastBody` matches.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_UnreadFirst` — bob has unread, carol's last is more recent but read; assert bob comes first.
- `TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_RenamedCounterparty` — insert wb, then `UPDATE users SET user_id = …` for the counterparty; assert `UserIDStr` reflects the new name (validates the JOIN to `users`, not the denormalized `from_userid`).
- `TestWaterBalloons_MarkConversationRead_OnlyInbound` — alice→bob and bob→alice rows; alice calls MarkConversationRead(viewer=alice, counterparty=bob); only the bob→alice rows get `read_at` set. Critical: don't accidentally mark messages alice *sent* as read (they're already read by definition, but the `to_user_id` filter must hold or we'd be writing to wrong rows).
- `TestWaterBalloons_MarkConversationRead_Idempotent` — call twice, no error, row count unchanged on second call.
- `TestWaterBalloons_GetByID` — round-trip an inserted row.

### 6b. TUI: new `internal/tui/screen_wb_test.go`

Build helpers analogous to `mailFixture` (`screen_mail_test.go:14-27`):

```go
func wbFixture(t *testing.T, seed int) (Deps, *store.User /*alice*/) { … }
```

Tests:

- `TestWBInbox_LoadsCounterparties` — seed 3 wb from alice, expect 1 counterparty row.
- `TestWBInbox_NoAutoMarkRead` — seed 3 unread, construct inbox, assert `ListUnreadFor(bob)` still returns 3 (regression for the `MarkAllReadFor` removal).
- `TestWBInbox_BackKeys` — esc/backspace/left/h/Q → ScreenMainMenu (mirrors mail pattern).
- `TestWBInbox_OpenThread` — Enter/space/l/right → `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: alice.ID}`.
- `TestWBInbox_ComposeKey` — `c` → `ScreenWBCompose` with no recipient.
- `TestWBInbox_NoopsWhenEmpty` — empty list, `enter`/`l` are no-ops.
- `TestWBThread_LoadsBothDirections` — bob→alice (2) and alice→bob (2) seeded; expect 4 items in ASC id order.
- `TestWBThread_OpeningMarksReadInbound` — pre-condition unread=2 from bob; open thread; post-condition unread=0; AND alice's outbound msgs untouched (their `read_at` should not be set by the thread-open path).
- `TestWBThread_BackKeys` — esc/backspace/left/h → ScreenWBInbox.
- `TestWBThread_QuitKey` — Q → ScreenMainMenu.
- `TestWBThread_ComposeKey` — `c` and `r` both → ScreenWBCompose with `Recipient=alice.UserID`.
- `TestWBThread_LiveAppendOnIncoming` — construct thread (alice viewing, counterparty=bob), feed `WBIncomingMsg{FromUserID: "bob", ...}` via Update, expect items grew by 1 and the new one is marked read in DB.
- `TestWBThread_LiveAppendIgnoresOtherCounterparty` — same setup, feed `WBIncomingMsg{FromUserID: "carol", …}`, expect items unchanged.
- `TestNavigate_WBThreadCarriesCounterpartyID` (in `root_test.go`) — verify Root.navigate constructs `wbThreadModel` with the right ID.

## 7. Verification (manual, two SSH sessions)

1. Start server: `make run` (or whichever target wraps `./sshbbs`).
2. Terminal A: `ssh alice@localhost -p 2222`. Terminal B: `ssh bob@localhost -p 2222`.
3. **Build history while both online:**
   - In B: Ctrl+U (open inbox) → `c` (compose) → To: `alice`, Body: `hi from bob`. Ctrl+S.
   - In A: see toast `💧 bob: hi from bob`. (Already auto-marked read because online.)
   - In A: Ctrl+U → `c` → To: `bob`, Body: `hey bob`. Ctrl+S.
   - Repeat with body `another` to give the thread depth.
4. **Build offline unread:**
   - Disconnect A.
   - In B: Ctrl+U → `c` → To: `alice`, Body: `bob unread 1`. Repeat with `bob unread 2`.
5. **Reconnect alice:**
   - `ssh alice@localhost -p 2222`. The drain-on-connect (`server.go:138-148`) replays both messages as toasts, marking them read **and** keeping them in the conversation history.
6. **Phase 1 inbox:** Alice presses Ctrl+U. Expect: one row, `bob`, unread `0` (drain already cleared them), preview shows the most recent body. *If we instead want fresh-on-reconnect to show as unread, we'd suppress the immediate `MarkRead` in `server.go:146` and rely on the thread to mark them — but that's a separate decision; out of scope for Phase 1.*
7. **Drill into thread:** Alice presses Enter on the bob row. Expect: scrollback showing all 5 messages in chronological order, sender prefix correct (you/bob), body wraps reasonably, scroll with j/k.
8. **Read-marking verification:** Quit and reconnect alice — no toasts replay (DB has them all read).
9. **Live append with thread open:** Alice in thread with bob. Bob sends `live test`. Expect toast PLUS the row appearing at the bottom of the thread without re-entering. Run `sqlite3 sshbbs.db "SELECT id, body, read_at FROM water_balloons ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 5"` and confirm the new row's `read_at` is set.
10. **Counterparty roll-up correctness:** Have a third user `carol` send to alice. Alice's inbox should now show 2 rows, carol on top (unread first), bob below (all read).
11. **Self-WB sanity check:** Alice tries `c` → To: `alice`. See concern #1 below — current behavior is "succeeds and shows up in your own inbox as a row from yourself." Note this; don't fix in Phase 1.

## 8. Concerns / edge cases

1. **Self-water-balloon is not blocked.** `screen_wb.go:234` does `GetByUserID(to)` — if `to == m.deps.User.UserID` it succeeds. Then `Insert` writes `from_user_id == to_user_id`. With Phase 1's CTE, the `cp_id` for that row is `to_user_id` (which equals viewer's own ID) — alice will see a "conversation with herself" row. Mostly harmless but weird. **Recommendation:** out of Phase 1 scope; add a one-line guard in `wbComposeModel.submit` later (`if target.ID == from.ID { m.err = "cannot send to yourself"; return m, nil }`). Same applies to mail (out of scope but worth a TODO).

2. **Threading with deleted users.** No user-delete code exists today (`grep -rn "DELETE FROM users"` returns nothing). The `to_user_id` and `from_user_id` columns have `REFERENCES users(id)` but no cascade. When delete eventually lands, the JOIN to `users` in `ListCounterpartiesFor` would silently drop those conversations from the inbox — surprising but not corrupt. Out of Phase 1 scope; if/when delete lands, switch to soft-delete or LEFT JOIN with a fallback handle. Worth a TODO comment in the new SQL.

3. **Renamed counterpart / `from_userid` denorm.** The `from_userid` column on `water_balloons` is a snapshot of the sender's handle at insert time. If alice renames to `alice2`, old rows still say `from_userid='alice'`. The `ListCounterpartiesFor` query JOINs `users` on the canonical id, so the inbox header always shows the *current* handle — good. But the *body* of `ListConversation` still returns old `FromUserIDStr` from the row itself, so the in-thread "alice: hi" prefix would say the old name. **Recommendation:** in the thread `View`, derive the prefix from `m.cpUserID` (current handle) for incoming and "you" for outgoing — don't trust `w.FromUserIDStr`. This is clean and free.

4. **Empty conversation case in `ListConversation`.** If you somehow open a thread for a counterparty with whom you have zero messages (shouldn't happen via the normal flow because the inbox only lists rows that exist), the screen should show an "(empty thread)" placeholder rather than blowing up. Mirror `screen_mail.go:220-223`.

5. **Concurrency on `MarkConversationRead`.** Holds `writeMu` correctly. But the live-append path in §3c also calls `MarkRead` — if a second `WBIncomingMsg` arrives while the constructor is still running its `MarkConversationRead`, both calls take the mutex, no race. Tests should run with `-race` (already standard, per the conventions section).

6. **`CounterpartyUserID` zero check in `Root.navigate`.** If a buggy caller emits `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBThread, CounterpartyUserID: 0}`, the constructor will get `users.GetByID(0)` → `ErrUserNotFound`. The screen will show `loadErr` — acceptable, not a crash. Don't bother adding a guard.

7. **`unread_count` overflow in column rendering.** Counterparty unread is `int64`. If we ever have a chatty user with > 999 unread the column padding (`statusW=5`) handles it. No-op for now.

## 9. Sequencing (suggested)

Pure additive then surgical: each step compiles and tests should pass between steps, so a reviewer can land in pieces.

1. **Repo + tests first.** Add `WBCounterparty`, `ListCounterpartiesFor`, `ListConversation`, `MarkConversationRead`, `GetByID`. Land the repo tests. (No screen wired up yet — existing inbox still uses `ListInboxFor`. Build is green.)
2. **NavigateMsg field + screen constant.** `CounterpartyUserID` and `ScreenWBThread` added. Routing case in `root.go` returns the model. Compiles, untouched runtime behavior.
3. **Thread screen + tests.** `wbThreadModel` and `screen_wb_test.go`'s thread tests. (Inbox still flat.)
4. **Inbox swap + tests.** Replace flat-list inbox with counterparty inbox. Remove `MarkAllReadFor` line. Update inbox tests (rename, change expectations).
5. **Live append in thread + test.** The `WBIncomingMsg` case in `wbThreadModel.Update`.
6. **Optional online-list `t` shortcut.** One Update case + help-text update + one test.
