Phase 1 shipped + verified. Summary:

**Code changes** (no schema migration needed — `water_balloons` already had what we need):
- `internal/store/waterballoons.go` — added `WBCounterparty` + `ListCounterpartiesFor` + `ListConversation` + `MarkConversationRead` + `GetByID`. The CTE in `ListCounterpartiesFor` reads `r.created_at` from the JOIN'd row (resolved via `MAX(id)`) instead of `MAX(created_at)` because modernc/sqlite drops the time.Time type hint across aggregates.
- `internal/tui/messages.go` — `ScreenWBThread` const + `CounterpartyUserID int64` field.
- `internal/tui/root.go` — navigate case.
- `internal/tui/screen_wb.go` — inbox rewired to counterparty roll-up (auto-mark-read removed), new `wbThreadModel` with mark-read-on-enter + `WBIncomingMsg` live-append handler.
- `internal/tui/screen_online.go` — `t` opens thread; help line updated.

**Tests** (all green under `-race`, full repo suite):
- 11 new repo tests (conversation directionality, counterparty grouping, unread counting, last-from-me direction, unread-first ordering, handle-via-JOIN, MarkConversationRead inbound-only + idempotent, GetByID).
- 13 new TUI tests in `screen_wb_test.go` (inbox roll-up, no-auto-mark-read regression, thread back/quit/compose keys, mark-read-inbound-only, current-handle render, empty placeholder, live-append match + ignore-other-counterparty).
- 2 new tests in `screen_online_test.go` (`t` opens thread, no-op when empty).

**Manual verification** still up to you — the plan file at `.claude/plans/jiggly-hopping-wolf.md` has 10 steps for two SSH sessions (alice/bob/carol). Wanted highlights: (4) inbox shows one row per counterparty with unread counts, (5) Enter drills into chronological both-direction history, (7) live-append works while sitting in the thread, (10) `t` from online list opens DM history directly.

**Phase 2 (DM window)** added to `TODO.md` as P2/[M] — pulls in textinput at the bottom of `wbThreadModel`, send-via-existing-path, plus toast suppression when the active screen matches.

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>

Commit Phase 1

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Ship 水球 conversation threads: counterparty roll-up + bidirectional history

Inbox switches from a flat per-message list to a per-counterparty roll-up
(unread count + last-message preview); Enter drills into a chronological
two-direction transcript with that user. Mark-read moves from "open inbox"
to "open this thread", so seeing your inbox no longer ack-clears unread
badges. Online list gains 't' as a chat-style entry point distinct from
Enter (compose).

No schema migration — `water_balloons` already had everything needed; the
new repo methods (`ListCounterpartiesFor`, `ListConversation`,
`MarkConversationRead`, `GetByID`) wrap it. CTE in `ListCounterpartiesFor`
reads `r.created_at` from the JOIN'd row (resolved via `MAX(id)`) instead
of `MAX(created_at)` because modernc/sqlite drops the time.Time type hint
across aggregates. Counterparty handle comes from a JOIN on `users.id` so
a renamed handle reads correctly rather than echoing the `from_userid`
snapshot stored at insert time.

Live append: `wbThreadModel.Update` handles `WBIncomingMsg` from the
broker — fixes the bug where alice viewing the bob thread saw the toast
but the thread didn't update until re-entry. Toast suppression deferred

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"fmt"
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
)

// HelpEntry is one row in a screen's keymap. Keys is the displayed shortcut
// (e.g. "Enter/→/l"), Desc the human-readable action.
type HelpEntry struct {
	Keys string
	Desc string
}

// helpHeader is the optional title row shown above the keymap. Empty for
// most screens; some screens use it to clarify mode-specific keys.
type helpSection struct {
	Heading string
	Entries []HelpEntry
}

// screenHelp maps a Screen constant to the keys we want the help overlay
// to surface. Mirrors the inline help-line strings rendered at the bottom
// of each screen, with extra notes on globals (Ctrl+U, Ctrl+C, ?) so the
// overlay is self-contained.
//
// Form screens (compose, edit, password change, board-banner edit) are
// intentionally absent: they need to pass `?` through to textinput so the
// user can type it as a literal character. Their bottom help-line already
// surfaces every key they bind.
var screenHelp = map[Screen][]helpSection{
	ScreenMainMenu: {{
		Heading: "Main menu",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k", "move cursor"},
			{"Enter/→/l", "select"},
			{"1-9", "jump to numbered slot"},
			{"q", "quit"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenBoardList: {{
		Heading: "Board list",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k [/]", "move cursor"},
			{"Enter/→/l", "open board"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenBoardView: {{
		Heading: "Board view (article list)",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k [/]", "move cursor"},
			{"Enter/→/l", "open article"},
			{"p", "post new article (non-guest)"},
			{"b", "board banner splash (if banner set)"},
			{"B", "edit banner (mod+)"},
			{"M", "pin / unpin (mod+)"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to board list"},
			{"Q", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenArticleView: {{
		Heading: "Article view",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"j/k", "scroll"},
			{"g/G", "top / bottom"},
			{"PgUp/PgDn", "page scroll (b/space aliases)"},
			{"+ / - / =", "推 / 噓 / → (non-guest)"},
			{"p / P", "select push for delete"},
			{"D", "delete cursored article or push (owner / mod+)"},
			{"E", "edit article (author / mod+)"},
			{"y", "export article markdown"},
			{"[ / ]", "previous / next article on this board"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to board"},
			{"Q", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenWBInbox: {{
		Heading: "水球 inbox (per-counterparty roll-up)",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k", "move cursor"},
			{"Enter/→/l/r", "open thread with cursored user"},
			{"c", "compose new water balloon"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenWBThread: {{
		Heading: "水球 對話 (1-to-1 history)",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k", "scroll"},
			{"g / G", "top / end"},
			{"c / r", "compose to this user"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to inbox"},
			{"Q", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenOnline: {{
		Heading: "Online users",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k [/]", "move cursor"},
			{"Enter/→/l", "丟水球 (compose)"},
			{"t", "對話 thread (chat-style)"},
			{"g / G / Home / End", "top / bottom"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
			{"Q", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenMailInbox: {{
		Heading: "信箱 (mail inbox)",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k [/]", "move cursor"},
			{"Enter/→/l", "open thread"},
			{"r", "reply to cursored mail"},
			{"c", "compose new mail"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
			{"Q", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenMailThread: {{
		Heading: "信件 thread",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k", "scroll"},
			{"g", "top"},
			{"r", "reply"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to inbox"},
			{"Q", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenAdminUsers: {{
		Heading: "Admin: users",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"↑/↓ j/k", "move cursor"},
			{"[ / ]", "prev / next page"},
			{"a / m / u / g", "promote to admin / mod / user / guest"},
			{"R", "reset password (forces change on next login)"},
			{"Esc/←/h", "back to main menu"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenArticleExport: {{
		Heading: "Export article",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"1", "plain body"},
			{"2", "body + pushes"},
			{"3", "save to data/exports/<userid>/"},
			{"c", "OSC52 copy to clipboard"},
			{"Esc/←/h/q", "back"},
		},
	}},
	ScreenBoardSplash: {{
		Heading: "Board banner splash",
		Entries: []HelpEntry{
			{"any key", "return to board view"},
		},
	}},
}

// helpGlobals are the cross-screen keys shown on every help page so users
// don't need to discover them per screen.
var helpGlobals = []HelpEntry{
	{"?", "show this help (any key dismisses)"},
	{"Ctrl+U", "open 水球 inbox (logged-in only)"},
	{"Ctrl+C", "disconnect"},
}

// isFormScreen reports whether a screen is a text-entry form whose
// textinput / textarea must receive '?' as a literal character. The help
// overlay is suppressed on these screens; the bottom help-line each form
// already renders is the source of truth there.
func isFormScreen(s Screen) bool {
	switch s {
	case ScreenPostCompose,
		ScreenWBCompose,
		ScreenMailCompose,
		ScreenArticleEdit,
		ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
		ScreenPasswordChange:
		return true
	}
	return false
}

// helpAvailable reports whether the help overlay should respond to '?' on
// this screen. False for form screens and during register/MustChangePassword
// (those flows start before any Screen const is meaningful).
func helpAvailable(deps Deps, s Screen) bool {
	if deps.IsRegister || deps.MustChangePassword {
		return false
	}
	return !isFormScreen(s)
}

// helpBox is the lipgloss frame around the help body. Width is set per-call
// so the box hugs the rendered content rather than spanning the terminal.
var helpBox = lipgloss.NewStyle().
	Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
	BorderForeground(lipgloss.Color("63")).
	Padding(1, 2)

// renderHelp builds the full-screen help overlay for the given screen.
// Includes per-screen entries (or a fallback message) plus the globals.
func renderHelp(s Screen) string {
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 鍵盤捷徑說明 Help ") + "\n\n")

	sections, ok := screenHelp[s]
	if !ok {
		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(no per-screen keymap registered for this screen)") + "\n\n")
	} else {
		for i, sec := range sections {
			if i > 0 {
				b.WriteString("\n")
			}
			if sec.Heading != "" {
				b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  "+sec.Heading) + "\n")
			}
			for _, e := range sec.Entries {
				b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
			}
		}
		b.WriteString("\n")
	}

	b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render("  Global") + "\n")
	for _, e := range helpGlobals {
		b.WriteString(formatHelpRow(e) + "\n")
	}

	b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to dismiss"))

	return helpBox.Render(b.String())
}

// formatHelpRow pads keys to a stable column width so the descriptions
// align — uses the runewidth-aware PadRight because keys often contain
// arrows / CJK glyphs.
func formatHelpRow(e HelpEntry) string {
	const keysW = 22
	return fmt.Sprintf("    %s  %s", PadRight(e.Keys, keysW), e.Desc)
}

```

```go
package tui

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

// rootForHelp builds a minimal Root logged in as alice on the main menu.
// Used by every help-overlay test below.
func rootForHelp(t *testing.T) Root {
	t.Helper()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
	deps := Deps{Store: st, User: alice, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
	return NewRoot(deps)
}

// TestHelp_QuestionMarkOpensOverlay asserts '?' on the main menu flips
// helpVisible and that View() returns the help block instead of the menu.
func TestHelp_QuestionMarkOpensOverlay(t *testing.T) {
	r := rootForHelp(t)
	if r.helpVisible {
		t.Fatal("helpVisible should start false")
	}
	updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
	r = updated.(Root)
	if !r.helpVisible {
		t.Errorf("? did not flip helpVisible")
	}
	v := r.View()
	if !strings.Contains(v, "Help") {
		t.Errorf("View missing help title; got: %q", v)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(v, "Main menu") {
		t.Errorf("View missing main-menu section heading; got: %q", v)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(v, "? ") || !strings.Contains(v, "Global") {
		t.Errorf("View missing global section / '?' entry; got: %q", v)
	}
}

// TestHelp_AnyKeyDismisses fires a non-'?' key while help is open and
// expects helpVisible to flip back off without triggering navigation.
func TestHelp_AnyKeyDismisses(t *testing.T) {
	r := rootForHelp(t)
	updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
	r = updated.(Root)
	if !r.helpVisible {
		t.Fatal("setup: ? should open help")
	}

	for _, key := range []string{"j", "esc", "enter", " ", "x"} {
		t.Run(key, func(t *testing.T) {
			r2 := r // copy
			r2.helpVisible = true
			updated, cmd := r2.Update(keyOf(key))
			r2 = updated.(Root)
			if r2.helpVisible {
				t.Errorf("key %q did not dismiss help overlay", key)
			}
			if cmd != nil {
				if msg := runCmd(cmd); msg != nil {
					t.Errorf("dismissing key %q produced %T %+v, want nil", key, msg, msg)
				}
			}
		})
	}
}

// TestHelp_CtrlCStillQuitsWhileOpen guards against a regression where the
// help overlay swallows Ctrl+C. Ctrl+C must always quit.
func TestHelp_CtrlCStillQuitsWhileOpen(t *testing.T) {
	r := rootForHelp(t)
	updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
	r = updated.(Root)
	if !r.helpVisible {
		t.Fatal("setup")
	}
	_, cmd := r.Update(keyOf("ctrl+c"))
	if cmd == nil {
		t.Fatal("ctrl+c returned no cmd")
	}
	got := runCmd(cmd)
	if _, ok := got.(tea.QuitMsg); !ok {
		t.Errorf("ctrl+c → %T %+v, want tea.QuitMsg", got, got)
	}
}

// TestHelp_ActiveScreenTrackedAcrossNavigate asserts navigate updates the
// activeScreen field so subsequent '?' shows the correct page.
func TestHelp_ActiveScreenTrackedAcrossNavigate(t *testing.T) {
	r := rootForHelp(t)
	if r.activeScreen != ScreenMainMenu {
		t.Errorf("initial activeScreen = %v, want ScreenMainMenu", r.activeScreen)
	}
	updated, _ := r.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
	r = updated.(Root)
	if r.activeScreen != ScreenWBInbox {
		t.Errorf("after navigate to ScreenWBInbox: activeScreen = %v", r.activeScreen)
	}

	// '?' on the WB inbox should mention water balloons.
	updated, _ = r.Update(keyOf("?"))
	r = updated.(Root)
	if !strings.Contains(r.View(), "水球") {
		t.Errorf("WB inbox help missing 水球 heading; got: %q", r.View())
	}
}

// TestHelp_FormScreensSuppressOverlay verifies '?' is forwarded to the
// sub-screen (not intercepted) when a form screen is active. We assert
// helpVisible stays false; the textinput within the sub will receive the
// keypress and treat it as input.
func TestHelp_FormScreensSuppressOverlay(t *testing.T) {
	formScreens := []Screen{
		ScreenPostCompose,
		ScreenWBCompose,
		ScreenMailCompose,
		ScreenArticleEdit,
		ScreenBoardBannerEdit,
		ScreenPasswordChange,
	}
	for _, s := range formScreens {
		t.Run(s.String(), func(t *testing.T) {
			r := rootForHelp(t)
			r.activeScreen = s
			updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
			r2 := updated.(Root)
			if r2.helpVisible {
				t.Errorf("form screen %v: '?' should not open help overlay", s)
			}
		})
	}
}

// TestHelp_RegisterFlowSuppresses ensures '?' during register doesn't
// pop a help overlay (the user isn't logged in; help table is irrelevant).
func TestHelp_RegisterFlowSuppresses(t *testing.T) {
	st := storetest.New(t)
	deps := Deps{Store: st, IsRegister: true}
	r := NewRoot(deps)
	updated, _ := r.Update(keyOf("?"))
	r = updated.(Root)
	if r.helpVisible {
		t.Errorf("'?' in register flow should not open help overlay")
	}
}

// TestHelp_NonFormScreensAllHaveEntries is a coverage check: every
// non-form, user-facing screen const should appear in the screenHelp map
// so the overlay never falls back to the "no keymap registered" message.
func TestHelp_NonFormScreensAllHaveEntries(t *testing.T) {
	want := []Screen{
		ScreenMainMenu,
		ScreenBoardList,
		ScreenBoardView,
		ScreenArticleView,
		ScreenWBInbox,
		ScreenWBThread,
		ScreenOnline,
		ScreenMailInbox,
		ScreenMailThread,
		ScreenAdminUsers,
		ScreenArticleExport,
		ScreenBoardSplash,
	}
	for _, s := range want {
		if _, ok := screenHelp[s]; !ok {
			t.Errorf("missing screenHelp entry for %v", s)
		}
	}
}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:07:58Z)**_

Now Phase 2 plan + implement. Plan first:

**Phase 2 design** (refining the sketch in `.claude/plans/jiggly-hopping-wolf.md`):

- **Layout**: scrollback uses `m.height - 6` lines; single-line `textinput` (240-char cap, matching wb compose) below it; status/help on the bottom.
- **Default focus = input** (chat app convention). `Tab` toggles to scrollback for vim-style scrolling.
- **Key dispatch by focus**:
  - **Input focused**: `Esc` → back to inbox, `Tab` → scrollback, `Up/Down/PgUp/PgDn` → scroll (single-line textinput doesn't use these), `Enter` → send, anything else → textinput. (No `h/l/Backspace/Q` interception — those are typed as chars.)
  - **Scrollback focused**: `Esc/Backspace/Left/h` → back, `Tab` → input, `Up/k/PgUp` `Down/j/PgDn` → scroll, `g/G` → top/end, `Q` → main menu.
- **Send path**: re-use exact same flow as `wbComposeModel.submit` — `Insert(viewer→cp, body, false)` then `Broker.Send(cpID, WBIncomingMsg)`. On success: append the row locally (no re-fetch — the caller side has nothing to wait for), clear input, auto-scroll to bottom. On error: inline `m.err` line above input, cleared on next keystroke.
- **Drop `c`/`r`** from the thread keymap — input box subsumes them. Compose-to-anyone-else stays accessible via inbox `c`.
- **Toast suppression**: Root.Update on `WBIncomingMsg` — if `activeScreen == ScreenWBThread` and the active sub is a `wbThreadModel` whose `cpUserID` matches `msg.FromUserID`, skip the toast. Add helper method `wbThreadModel.matchesCounterparty(handle string) bool` + type-assertion in Root.
- **Help map**: update `ScreenWBThread` entries to reflect input-focused keys.

**Tests**: `TestWBThread_SendOnEnter` (Insert + broker fan-out + local append + input cleared); `TestWBThread_SendEmptyNoop`; `TestWBThread_TabTogglesFocus`; `TestWBThread_EscFromInputGoesBack` (no character forwarded to textinput); `TestRoot_ToastSuppressedInMatchingThread`; `TestRoot_ToastFiresInOtherThread`.

Implementing now.

```text
295	
296	// =====================================================================
297	// Thread (1-to-1 conversation history with one counterparty)
298	// =====================================================================
299	
300	type wbThreadModel struct {
301		deps     Deps
302		cpID     int64  // counterparty user.id
303		cpUserID string // counterparty current handle (from users JOIN, not from_userid snapshot)
304		items    []*store.WaterBalloon
305		scroll   int
306		width    int
307		height   int
308		loadErr  error
309	}
310	
311	func newWBThreadModel(deps Deps, counterpartyID int64) wbThreadModel {
312		ctx := context.Background()
313		cp, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(ctx, counterpartyID)
314		if err != nil {
315			return wbThreadModel{deps: deps, cpID: counterpartyID, loadErr: err}
316		}
317		items, err := deps.Store.WaterBalloons().ListConversation(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID, 200)
318		if err == nil {
319			// Mark only the inbound side as read on entering the thread.
320			// Replaces the old "open inbox = ack everything" behaviour.
321			_ = deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkConversationRead(ctx, deps.User.ID, counterpartyID)
322			// Reflect the mark in our local copy so View doesn't show stale "NEW".
323			now := time.Now()
324			for _, w := range items {
325				if w.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !w.ReadAt.Valid {
326					w.ReadAt = sql.NullTime{Time: now, Valid: true}
327				}
328			}
329		}
330		return wbThreadModel{
331			deps:     deps,
332			cpID:     counterpartyID,
333			cpUserID: cp.UserID,
334			items:    items,
335			loadErr:  err,
336		}
337	}
338	
339	func (m wbThreadModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
340	
341	func (m wbThreadModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
342		switch msg := msg.(type) {
343		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
344			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
345			return m, nil
346		case WBIncomingMsg:
347			// Live append: when the broker fans out a wb from our counterparty
348			// while we're viewing this thread, fold it in instead of waiting
349			// for re-entry. Toast still fires from Root (Phase 1 keeps both;
350			// toast suppression is Phase 2 territory).
351			if !strings.EqualFold(msg.FromUserID, m.cpUserID) {
352				return m, nil
353			}
354			ctx := context.Background()
355			if wb, err := m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().GetByID(ctx, msg.ID); err == nil {
356				m.items = append(m.items, wb)
357				// Auto-scroll to the new bottom so the user sees the message.
358				m.scroll = 1 << 30
359			}
360			_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(ctx, msg.ID)
361			return m, nil
362		case tea.KeyMsg:
363			switch msg.String() {
364			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
365				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox} }
366			case "Q":
367				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
368			case "up", "k":
369				if m.scroll > 0 {
370					m.scroll--
371				}
372			case "down", "j":
373				m.scroll++ // clamped at render time
374			case "g", "home":
375				m.scroll = 0
376			case "G", "end":
377				m.scroll = 1 << 30 // clamped at render time
378			case "c", "r":
379				cp := m.cpUserID
380				return m, func() tea.Msg {
381					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBCompose, Recipient: cp}
382				}
383			}
384		}
385		return m, nil
386	}
387	
388	func (m wbThreadModel) View() string {
389		var b strings.Builder
390		b.WriteString("\n")
391		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 對話 with %s ", m.cpUserID)))
392		b.WriteString("\n\n")
393	
394		if m.loadErr != nil {
395			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
396			return b.String()
397		}
398		if len(m.items) == 0 {
399			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(empty thread — press c to start)") + "\n")
400			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("c compose · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
401			return b.String()
402		}
403	
404		lines := m.renderLines()
405		visible := m.height - 5
406		if visible < 1 {
407			visible = len(lines)
408		}
409		if m.scroll > len(lines)-visible {
410			m.scroll = max(0, len(lines)-visible)
411		}
412		end := min(m.scroll+visible, len(lines))
413		for _, ln := range lines[m.scroll:end] {
414			b.WriteString(ln + "\n")
415		}
416		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("↑/↓ j/k scroll · g/G top/end · c/r reply · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
417		return b.String()
418	}
419	
420	// renderLines flattens the message list to display lines so scroll math is
421	// straightforward. Each message contributes a header line + body lines + a
422	// blank separator. Body wrapping is left to the terminal (240-char cap on
423	// compose keeps it short in practice).
424	func (m wbThreadModel) renderLines() []string {
```

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Ship 水球 DM window (Phase 2): inline textinput + toast suppression

The 1-to-1 thread view (Phase 1) becomes a chat-style DM. Scrollback on
top, single-line textinput at the bottom, default focus on input. Tab
toggles to scrollback for vim-style scrolling. Enter sends; Esc backs
out to the inbox. While the input is focused, only Esc/Enter/Tab and
arrow / PgUp / PgDn are intercepted — the rest (h, j, k, l, q, Q, c, r,
punctuation) is typed as characters. Tab into the scrollback restores
the full Phase 1 keymap (Esc/←/h/Q/g/G/j/k).

Send path mirrors `wbComposeModel.submit` exactly so persistence and
broker delivery behaviour stay identical: Insert, then Broker.Send,
then on success append the row locally and clear the input. No "✓ sent"
page transition — the appended row is the visual confirmation.

Toast suppression in Root.Update: when activeScreen is ScreenWBThread
AND the active sub-model's cpUserID matches the incoming wb's sender,
the 💧 toast is skipped (the thread itself already shows the row, so
the toast would be duplicate noise). Scoped via a `matchesCounterparty`
method + type assertion. Toasts still fire on every other screen and
for non-matching counterparties — covered by `TestRoot_ToastFiresIn
OtherThread` and `TestRoot_ToastFiresOnNonThreadScreens`.

Help overlay (the `?` feature) updated with two-section keymap for the
thread reflecting input-focus vs scrollback-focus modes.

The `c` and `r` thread keys (which navigated to ScreenWBCompose
pre-filled) are removed — input box subsumes them. Compose-to-anyone
stays accessible via inbox `c`.

Tests: 7 new on the thread (Tab cycles focus, send-on-Enter persists +
clears + appends, empty Enter no-op, typed 'h' doesn't navigate while
input focused, focus-aware back / quit) + 3 in root_test.go
(suppression scope: matching → suppress, other-cp → toast, non-thread
screen → toast). All green under `go test -race ./...`.

TODO.md promotes Phase 1, Help overlay, and Phase 2 entries to Done
in the same commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:12:59Z)**_

Three commits landed:

- **db2fbb1** — `Ship 水球 conversation threads: counterparty roll-up + bidirectional history` (Phase 1 from earlier)
- **7c0fb42** — `Ship '?' help overlay: context-aware keymap per screen`
- **deaa6bc** — `Ship 水球 DM window (Phase 2): inline textinput + toast suppression`

**Help overlay** (`?`): full-screen, per-screen keymap from a `screenHelp` table indexed by `Screen` const. Form screens (compose / edit) suppress so they can take `?` as a literal. Ctrl+C still escapes if you've got it open. Discoverability: main menu's bottom help-line gained `· ? help`.

**DM Phase 2**: `wbThreadModel` now has scrollback above + 240-char `textinput` below, default focus on input. Tab toggles to scrollback for vim-style scrolling (full Phase 1 keymap restored there); while input is focused, only `Esc/Enter/Tab/Up/Down/PgUp/PgDn` are intercepted — `h/j/k/l/q/Q/c/r` etc. are typed as chars. Enter goes through the same `Insert + Broker.Send` flow as wb compose, so persistence + offline replay are unchanged. Toast suppressed in Root when the active thread's `cpUserID` matches the incoming sender — proven scoped via two contrast tests (other-cp still toasts, non-thread screens still toast).

All green under `go test -race ./...`. Manual smoke per the plan file's verification (`alice ↔ bob` two SSH sessions) covers the new flow: open thread → type → Enter sends inline, no page transition; `?` from any list screen shows that screen's keymap; toast doesn't fire when you're already viewing the matching thread.

```text
1	// Package chat is the in-memory hub that connects live SSH sessions for
2	// real-time features (water balloons, push broadcasts). It mirrors PTT's
3	// `userinfo_t` shared-memory table conceptually, but as a process-local
4	// map[user_id][]*Session — no SHM, no daemons.
5	package chat
6	
7	import (
8		"sync"
9	
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	)
12	
13	// Sender is the minimal slice of *tea.Program the broker actually needs.
14	// Decoupling lets tests substitute a recording fake without spinning up a
15	// real bubbletea program.
16	type Sender interface {
17		Send(tea.Msg)
18	}
19	
20	// Session is one live SSH connection. The same user may have multiple
21	// Sessions (PTT calls this "雙開"); the broker keeps them all and
22	// broadcasts to each.
23	type Session struct {
24		UserID    int64
25		UserIDStr string
26		Program   Sender
27	}
28	
29	// OnlineUser is the externally-visible view of a logged-in user.
30	type OnlineUser struct {
31		UserID    int64
32		UserIDStr string
33		Sessions  int
34	}
35	
36	type Broker struct {
37		mu       sync.RWMutex
38		sessions map[int64][]*Session
39	}
40	
41	func NewBroker() *Broker {
42		return &Broker{sessions: make(map[int64][]*Session)}
43	}
44	
45	func (b *Broker) Register(s *Session) {
46		b.mu.Lock()
47		defer b.mu.Unlock()
48		b.sessions[s.UserID] = append(b.sessions[s.UserID], s)
49	}
50	
51	// Unregister removes a single session for the given user (matched by
52	// Sender identity). If it was the last session, the user disappears from
53	// the online list.
54	func (b *Broker) Unregister(userID int64, prog Sender) {
55		b.mu.Lock()
56		defer b.mu.Unlock()
57		list := b.sessions[userID]
58		for i, s := range list {
59			if s.Program == prog {
60				b.sessions[userID] = append(list[:i], list[i+1:]...)
61				break
62			}
63		}
64		if len(b.sessions[userID]) == 0 {
65			delete(b.sessions, userID)
66		}
67	}
68	
69	// Send delivers msg to every live session of toUID. Returns true if at
70	// least one session received it (i.e. the recipient is online).
71	func (b *Broker) Send(toUID int64, msg tea.Msg) bool {
72		b.mu.RLock()
73		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
74		list := b.sessions[toUID]
75		for _, s := range list {
76			s.Program.Send(msg)
77		}
78		return len(list) > 0
79	}
80	
81	// SendToAll broadcasts to every online session. excludeUID lets the
82	// originator skip themselves (pass 0 to broadcast to everyone).
83	func (b *Broker) SendToAll(excludeUID int64, msg tea.Msg) {
84		b.mu.RLock()
85		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
86		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
87			if uid == excludeUID {
88				continue
89			}
90			for _, s := range list {
91				s.Program.Send(msg)
92			}
93		}
94	}
95	
96	// LookupByUserID returns the numeric user_id for a logged-in account name,
97	// or 0 if nobody by that name is currently online. Case-insensitive.
98	func (b *Broker) LookupByUserID(name string) int64 {
99		b.mu.RLock()
100		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
101		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
102			for _, s := range list {
103				if equalFold(s.UserIDStr, name) {
104					return uid
105				}
106			}
107		}
108		return 0
109	}
110	
111	func (b *Broker) OnlineList() []OnlineUser {
112		b.mu.RLock()
113		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
114		out := make([]OnlineUser, 0, len(b.sessions))
115		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
116			if len(list) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			out = append(out, OnlineUser{
120				UserID:    uid,
121				UserIDStr: list[0].UserIDStr,
122				Sessions:  len(list),
123			})
124		}
125		return out
126	}
127	
128	// SessionsSnapshot returns a defensive copy of every session pointer for
129	// graceful shutdown (so the caller can iterate without holding the lock
130	// while blocking on per-program quits).
131	func (b *Broker) SessionsSnapshot() []*Session {
132		b.mu.RLock()
133		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
134		var out []*Session
135		for _, list := range b.sessions {
136			out = append(out, list...)
137		}
138		return out
139	}
140	
141	// equalFold is strings.EqualFold inlined to avoid the import in this tiny package.
142	func equalFold(a, b string) bool {
143		if len(a) != len(b) {
144			return false
145		}
146		for i := 0; i < len(a); i++ {
147			ca, cb := a[i], b[i]
148			if ca >= 'A' && ca <= 'Z' {
149				ca += 32
150			}
151			if cb >= 'A' && cb <= 'Z' {
152				cb += 32
153			}
154			if ca != cb {
155				return false
156			}
157		}
158		return true
159	}
160
```
</details></tool-use>

package tea // import "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

func (p *Program) Send(msg Msg)
    Send sends a message to the main update function, effectively allowing
    messages to be injected from outside the program for interoperability
    purposes.

    If the program hasn't started yet this will be a blocking operation. If the
    program has already been terminated this will be a no-op, so it's safe to
    send messages after the program has exited.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:18:59Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:19:04Z)**_

**Root cause identified.** Bubbletea v1.3.10's `Program.Send` writes to an unbuffered channel (`tea.go:244`: `msgs: make(chan Msg)`). When alice's `wbComposeModel.submit` calls `Broker.Send(alice.ID, ...)` while alice's own program loop is blocked inside `Update`, the channel push deadlocks — there's no goroutine reading the other end until Update returns, which can never happen because submit is waiting on the push.

Same trap exists in `wbThreadModel.submit` — typing in your own DM and pressing Enter would deadlock too.

Two fixes layered: (1) defensive broker change so the deadlock can't recur, (2) block self-WB at the application layer (better UX — sending water balloons to yourself is nonsense anyway). Plus inbox filter so existing self-rows don't surface as a "conversation with yourself".

1	package chat_test

4		"sync"

13	// fakeSender is a chat.Sender that records every message for assertion.
14	type fakeSender struct {
15		mu     sync.Mutex
16		got    []tea.Msg
17		id     int // for distinguishing "雙開" sessions
18	}
19	
20	func newFake(id int) *fakeSender { return &fakeSender{id: id} }
21	
22	func (f *fakeSender) Send(msg tea.Msg) {
23		f.mu.Lock()
24		defer f.mu.Unlock()
25		f.got = append(f.got, msg)
26	}
27	
28	func (f *fakeSender) Got() []tea.Msg {
29		f.mu.Lock()
30		defer f.mu.Unlock()
31		out := make([]tea.Msg, len(f.got))
32		copy(out, f.got)
33		return out
34	}
35	
36	func TestBroker_RegisterAppearsOnline(t *testing.T) {
37		b := chat.NewBroker()
38		s := newFake(1)
39		b.Register(&chat.Session{UserID: 1, UserIDStr: "alice", Program: s})
40	
41		online := b.OnlineList()
42		if len(online) != 1 {
43			t.Fatalf("got %d online, want 1", len(online))
44		}
45		if online[0].UserIDStr != "alice" || online[0].Sessions != 1 {
46			t.Errorf("online[0] = %+v", online[0])
47		}
48	}
49	
50	func TestBroker_UnregisterRemoves(t *testing.T) {
51		b := chat.NewBroker()
52		s := newFake(1)
53		b.Register(&chat.Session{UserID: 1, UserIDStr: "alice", Program: s})
54		b.Unregister(1, s)
55		if len(b.OnlineList()) != 0 {
56			t.Errorf("OnlineList not empty after Unregister")
57		}
58	}
59	
60	// 雙開: same user, two sessions. Send should reach both. Unregister one
61	// must keep the other online.
62	func TestBroker_MultipleSessionsSameUser(t *testing.T) {
63		b := chat.NewBroker()
64		s1 := newFake(1)
65		s2 := newFake(2)
66		b.Register(&chat.Session{UserID: 1, UserIDStr: "alice", Program: s1})
67		b.Register(&chat.Session{UserID: 1, UserIDStr: "alice", Program: s2})
68	
69		online := b.OnlineList()
70		if len(online) != 1 {
71			t.Fatalf("OnlineList: got %d entries, want 1 (one user, two sessions)", len(online))
72		}
73		if online[0].Sessions != 2 {
74			t.Errorf("Sessions = %d, want 2", online[0].Sessions)
75		}
76	
77		if !b.Send(1, "hello") {
78			t.Error("Send returned false but recipient is online")
79		}
80		for i, s := range []*fakeSender{s1, s2} {
81			if g := s.Got(); len(g) != 1 || g[0] != "hello" {
82				t.Errorf("session %d got %v, want [\"hello\"]", i, g)
83			}
84		}
85	
86		// Drop s1; s2 must remain.
87		b.Unregister(1, s1)

89			t.Error("Send returned false; s2 should still be online")
90		}
91		if len(s1.Got()) != 1 {
92			t.Errorf("s1 received message after Unregister; len=%d", len(s1.Got()))
93		}
94		if len(s2.Got()) != 2 {
95			t.Errorf("s2.Got = %v, want length 2", s2.Got())
96		}
97	}

137	// Concurrency stress: many Register / Unregister / Send running together.
138	// Run with `go test -race` to surface data races.
139	func TestBroker_Concurrency(t *testing.T) {
140		b := chat.NewBroker()
141		const workers = 16
142		const iters = 100
143	
144		var sent atomic.Int64
145	
146		var wg sync.WaitGroup
147		for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
148			wg.Add(1)
149			go func(id int64) {
150				defer wg.Done()
151				s := newFake(int(id))
152				for i := 0; i < iters; i++ {
153					b.Register(&chat.Session{UserID: id, UserIDStr: "u", Program: s})
154					b.Send(id, "x")
155					sent.Add(1)
156					b.Unregister(id, s)
157				}
158			}(int64(w + 1))
159		}
160		wg.Wait()
161	
162		if got := len(b.OnlineList()); got != 0 {
163			t.Errorf("OnlineList not empty after all unregisters: %d", got)
164		}
165		if sent.Load() != int64(workers*iters) {
166			t.Errorf("sent = %d, want %d", sent.Load(), workers*iters)
167		}
168	}

```md
---
title: Sending a water balloon to yourself hangs the SSH session (no further keys, can't relogin)
date: 2026-05-06
---

## Symptom

After typing your own userid into the 丟水球 compose form and pressing
Ctrl+S, the SSH session freezes:

- The compose screen stays on screen, "✓ sent" never appears.
- No further keystrokes are accepted (Esc, Ctrl+C, even ↑/↓ all do nothing).
- The Go process keeps running — `ps`/`top` show it alive, the listener
  socket is still bound — but **every existing AND new SSH session hangs**:
  newly logging in via `ssh alice@... -p 2222` connects but produces no
  TUI output; you have to `kill` the server.
- No panic, no goroutine dump (`SIGQUIT` to the server prints one — see
  Detection below).

The recipient field can be the literal userid (e.g. `david`) or any case
variation that resolves to the same `users.id` as the sender's. Mailing
yourself does NOT trigger this — only water balloons.

## Root cause

`tea.Program.Send(msg)` (bubbletea v1.3.10, `tea.go:244` and `tea.go:774-779`)
writes to a channel declared as:

\```go
msgs: make(chan Msg)   // unbuffered
\```

The send path inside `wbComposeModel.submit` (and identically inside
`wbThreadModel.submit` since Phase 2) calls:

\```go
m.deps.Broker.Send(target.ID, WBIncomingMsg{ID: wb.ID, ...})
\```

When `target.ID == from.ID`, the broker iterates over `b.sessions[target.ID]`
— which is the caller's OWN session — and does `s.Program.Send(msg)`. That
becomes a blocking `p.msgs <- msg` because:

1. The bubbletea program loop is currently INSIDE `Update(KeyMsg{Ctrl+S})`,
   processing the submit. It is the only goroutine that reads `p.msgs`.
2. Until `Update` returns, nobody reads the channel.
3. Until `p.msgs <- msg` succeeds, `Update` cannot return.

Classic deadlock. The lock isolates the affected session's goroutine, but
because wish multiplexes through a shared listener, other sessions stack
up behind whatever shared resource the hung goroutine still holds —
practically, the whole server stops servicing new TUI traffic.

`tea.Program.Send`'s `select { <-ctx.Done(); p.msgs <- msg }` only escapes
on program shutdown, which the user can't trigger from a frozen TUI.

## Workaround

Block self-targeting at every site that calls `Broker.Send`:

\```go
// internal/tui/screen_wb.go — wbComposeModel.submit
if target.ID == from.ID {
    m.err = "cannot send to yourself"
    return m, nil
}

// internal/tui/screen_wb.go — wbThreadModel.submit
if m.cpID == from.ID {
    m.err = "cannot send to yourself"
    return m, nil
}

// internal/tui/screen_wb.go — newWBThreadModel
if deps.User != nil && counterpartyID == deps.User.ID {
    return wbThreadModel{
        deps: deps, cpID: counterpartyID,
        loadErr: errors.New("cannot open thread with yourself"),
    }
}
\```

Filter pre-existing self rows out of the inbox roll-up so users don't
re-discover the trap by clicking on themselves:

\```sql
-- internal/store/waterballoons.go — ListCounterpartiesFor CTE
agg AS (
    SELECT cp_id, MAX(id) AS last_id, ...
    FROM related
    WHERE cp_id != ?           -- NEW: hide self-WBs
    GROUP BY cp_id
)
\```

Add a comment on `Broker.Send` documenting the invariant so the next
caller doesn't reinvent the trap from a different screen.

## Detection

If a future report sounds the same — server alive, no further input, no
new connections work — confirm via `SIGQUIT`:

\```
kill -QUIT <pid>
\```

You'll see a goroutine dump where one (or more) goroutines are blocked at:

\```
runtime.gopark ...
runtime.chansend ...
github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea.(*Program).Send ...
github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat.(*Broker).Send ...
github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/tui.wbComposeModel.submit ...
\```

The Send→Send→submit chain inside the same session is the smoking gun.

## Prevention

- **Never call `Broker.Send(toUID, ...)` with `toUID == sender's UserID`
  from inside any bubbletea Update path.** Document this on the broker
  method itself; future agents/screens grep for "Broker.Send" before
  adding a new caller.
- If a self-send semantic is ever genuinely wanted, refactor `Broker.Send`
  to spawn a goroutine per session (`go s.Program.Send(msg)`) — but that
  loses ordering guarantees relative to other broker traffic and forces
  the existing tests (`TestBroker_MultipleSessionsSameUser`,
  `TestBroker_SendToAllExcludesSender`) to switch from "assert
  immediately after Send" to an `eventually`-style wait. Worth it only if
  the use case demands it; today the app-layer guard is sufficient.
- Mail's `Broker.Send` after `Mail().Insert` could in principle hit the
  same trap if the UI ever lets you mail yourself. Currently `mailCompose`
  doesn't have an explicit guard but the inbox/thread doesn't surface
  mail-from-self as a discoverable action; if a "mail yourself a
  reminder" feature ever lands, mirror the wbCompose guard.

```

Commit fix

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix self-WB hang: block sender==recipient before Broker.Send

Reported: typing your own userid into 丟水球 compose and pressing Ctrl+S
freezes the SSH session — no further keys, new SSH logins also hang,
process stays alive. Root cause is bubbletea's unbuffered msgs channel
(v1.3.10 tea.go:244 `msgs: make(chan Msg)`): when wbComposeModel.submit
calls Broker.Send(target.ID, ...) with target.ID == from.ID, the broker
pushes WBIncomingMsg onto the caller's OWN p.msgs. The bubbletea program
loop is the only reader of that channel and is currently blocked inside
Update waiting for submit to return. Classic deadlock; the affected
session goroutine wedges and shared listener resources stack other new
connections behind it. SIGQUIT a hung server and the Send→Send→submit
chain shows up immediately in the goroutine dump.

Phase 2's wbThreadModel.submit had the identical trap — typing in a
self-thread and pressing Enter would hang the same way. Inbox showing
self as a counterparty (any pre-existing self-rows from before this fix)
was the discoverability path, so users could re-find the trap without
trying to compose to themselves explicitly.

Three layers of guard, plus a doc:

  1. wbComposeModel.submit refuses target.ID == from.ID with an inline
     "cannot send to yourself" error. No DB row, no broker call.
  2. wbThreadModel.submit mirrors the same guard (defensive — newWBThread
     Model already refuses construction).
  3. newWBThreadModel sets loadErr if counterpartyID == deps.User.ID so
     a stray NavigateMsg{CounterpartyUserID: self} can't reach the input
     screen.
  4. ListCounterpartiesFor SQL filters out self-WBs from the CTE so any
     pre-fix rows in the DB no longer surface as a counterparty in the
     inbox roll-up.

Broker.Send gains a doc comment naming the invariant so the next call
site grep'd for it doesn't reinvent the trap from a new screen. The
broker stays synchronous on purpose — switching to a goroutine-per-send
model would remove the trap defensively but trade away message ordering
and force the chat tests off "assert immediately after Send"; not worth
it when the app-layer guard is exhaustive.

Tests: TestWBCompose_BlocksSelfSend (the original report path),
TestWBThread_RefusesSelfThread (constructor guard),
TestWBThread_SubmitGuardsAgainstSelf (defensive submit guard),
TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_HidesSelf (SQL filter).

Pitfall write-up at pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md
captures the verbatim symptom, the SIGQUIT detection step, the channel-
buffer mechanics, and the "mail-yourself a reminder" cousin trap so a
future feature doesn't recreate it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:23:21Z)**_

Fix shipped (**592ffe2**).

**What hung the server**: bubbletea v1.3.10's `tea.Program.Send` writes to an **unbuffered** `msgs` channel (`tea.go:244`). When `wbComposeModel.submit` called `Broker.Send(target.ID, ...)` with `target.ID == from.ID`, the broker pushed onto your own session's `p.msgs` — but your program loop was blocked inside `Update` waiting for `submit` to return, which couldn't return until the push succeeded. Self-deadlock; new SSH connections piled up behind shared listener state. (Phase 2's DM had the identical bug — typing in a self-thread + Enter would deadlock too.)

**What I changed** (4 layers + a doc):

1. `wbComposeModel.submit` refuses `target.ID == from.ID` with inline "cannot send to yourself" — no DB write, no broker call.
2. `wbThreadModel.submit` mirrors the guard (defensive belt+braces).
3. `newWBThreadModel` sets `loadErr` if `counterpartyID == User.ID` so a stray `NavigateMsg` can't even reach the input.
4. `ListCounterpartiesFor` SQL filters self-rows from the CTE → any pre-fix self-WBs in your DB won't appear as a "conversation with yourself" in the inbox roll-up anymore.
5. `Broker.Send` gains an `IMPORTANT:` doc comment naming the invariant so the next caller can't reinvent the trap.

**Why not refactor the broker to goroutine-async**: would remove the trap defensively but trade message ordering for it and force the chat tests off "assert immediately after Send". The app-layer guard is exhaustive for now; if a future feature genuinely needs self-targeting, the pitfall doc spells out the refactor.

**Pitfall write-up**: `pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md` captures verbatim symptom, SIGQUIT detection step, channel mechanics, and the "mail-yourself a reminder" cousin trap.

**Tests**: 4 new (compose self-block, thread constructor refusal, thread submit defense, SQL filter). Full `go test -race ./...` green.

If you've already got self-WB rows from your bug-repro session, they're invisible from the UI now but still in `data/bbs.db` — harmless, but if you want them gone: `sqlite3 data/bbs.db "DELETE FROM water_balloons WHERE from_user_id = to_user_id;"`.

---

**架構上 self-WB 可以安全實作 (只要 target==self 時跳過 Broker.Send 就沒 deadlock 風險)。你偏好哪個方向?**

Options:
- **允許 self-WB 當備忘錄 (Recommended)** - Insert 照常, 但 target==self 時跳過 Broker.Send 並立即 MarkRead。 inbox 保留「與自己」這個對話項 (加上 📝 / 'yourself' marker), thread 可正常進入並滾動/打字/累積。 Slack/WeChat/Telegram 都是這模式, 是實裝有用的 feature。工程量: 拿掉各層 self-block + Slack 風格 marker 不到 50 行。
- **一律拒絕但拒得更早** - 保持「不許」但把各個入口都加上進關: online list Enter (選 self) 仍拒、compose 被帶 self recipient 進去就直接拒、compose 的 To 欄位在 Tab/Enter 離開時驗證。 你可能永遠看不到「進去 body 才跳 error」這種不一致的體驗。工程量相仿, 全部是 early-return 守衛。


**Answer:** 允許 self-WB 當備忘錄 (Recommended)" selected preview:
┌─ 水球 inbox ─────────────────┐
│ ▸ 📝 yourself  (memo)    1m │
│   alice        (3 unread)  10m │
│   bob          ·            2h │
└───────────────────────────────┘

┌─ chat with yourself 📝 ────────┐
│ 14:23 you: 進口鱔魚門口可樂要帶 │
│ 14:24 you: 1pm 開會別忘了      │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│ > _                            │
└─────────────────────────────┘

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit memo feature

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Allow self-WB as memo: skip Broker.Send, mark read, 📝 marker

Replaces the previous block-self-WB guards with a sidestep: when
target == sender, the compose / thread submit paths Insert into the DB
as usual but skip the Broker.Send call (the deadlock cause — see
pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md for why an unbuffered
msgs channel makes self-targeted Send fatal) and mark the row read
immediately so it doesn't replay as a toast on reconnect.

User intent (from the inconsistency report): "Enter from online list
opens compose, lets me type the body, then only blocks at Ctrl+S"
revealed that the previous defenses fired at different layers. Rather
than tighten the blocks (which would have meant duplicating "is self?"
checks at every navigate / construct site), the user opted to allow
self-DM as a memo feature — Slack/WeChat/Telegram precedent, real
shorthand for "remind me / paste my own clipboard / scratch buffer".

What changed:

- wbComposeModel.submit: branches on target.ID == from.ID; self-branch
  skips Broker.Send + MarkRead immediately; non-self path unchanged.
- wbThreadModel.submit: identical shape so DM-into-self-thread also
  works as memo.
- newWBThreadModel: drops the loadErr-on-self refusal; thread opens
  normally for self.
- ListCounterpartiesFor SQL: drops the `WHERE cp_id != ?` filter so
  self-rows appear in the inbox roll-up (the memo feature must be
  discoverable).
- Inbox row: when the counterparty is the viewer, the "With" column
  shows `📝 yourself` and the preview drops the redundant "you:" prefix.
- Thread header: when cpID == User.ID, header reads
  `對話 with 📝 yourself (memo)`.
- Pitfall doc rewritten: workaround section now describes the
  skip-broker-call pattern rather than the old refuse-the-action
  approach. Broker.Send doc comment unchanged — its invariant ("don't
  call with toUID == sender from inside an Update path") is what the
  fix obeys.

Tests flipped:

- TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_HidesSelf →
  TestWaterBalloons_ListCounterpartiesFor_ShowsSelf (asserts both self
  and bob appear).
- TestWBThread_RefusesSelfThread →
  TestWBThread_AllowsSelfThread (constructor accepts; View renders
  "yourself" marker).
- TestWBThread_SubmitGuardsAgainstSelf →
  TestWBThread_SelfSubmitSkipsBroker (proves DB row written + auto
  marked read; that pair only happens together when the broker path
  was skipped).
- TestWBCompose_BlocksSelfSend →
  TestWBCompose_AllowsSelfMemo (success path: cm.sent set, DB row
  exists, ReadAt is valid).

The "fix" for the original hang report (commit 592ffe2) is preserved
in spirit — self-targeting still never reaches Broker.Send. Only the
user-visible behaviour flipped from "error toast" to "stored as memo".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

 6 files changed, 554 insertions(+), 123 deletions(-)

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:35:00Z)**_

Shipped (**a3d5b21**).

**The change in spirit**: instead of *blocking* self-WB (which forced you to discover the inconsistency between `t` blocking early and `Enter→Ctrl+S` blocking late), the code now *sidesteps the dangerous part* and lets the harmless part through:

- `target == self` → Insert + immediate MarkRead + **skip `Broker.Send`** entirely. The deadlock-causing call is never made.
- `target != self` → unchanged.

Same code shape on both compose and thread submit paths, so there's no inconsistency between entry points. Inbox / thread / compose / online-list all work for self.

**Visible markers** so you can tell it apart from a real DM:

```
┌─ 水球 inbox ─────────────────┐
│ ▸ 📝 yourself  (memo)    1m │
│   alice        (3 unread) 10m│
│   bob          ·           2h│
└──────────────────────────────┘

對話 with 📝 yourself (memo)
```

Inbox preview drops the redundant `you:` prefix for self-rows since both ends are you.

**Tests flipped** (4 tests): `_HidesSelf`→`_ShowsSelf`, `_RefusesSelfThread`→`_AllowsSelfThread`, `_SubmitGuardsAgainstSelf`→`_SelfSubmitSkipsBroker`, `_BlocksSelfSend`→`_AllowsSelfMemo`. The new ones prove the load-bearing invariant: a self-WB ends up in the DB **with `read_at` set** — that pair only happens together when the broker path was skipped, so the test indirectly verifies the deadlock can't recur.

The pitfall doc is updated to describe the actual fix (skip-broker pattern, not refuse-the-action). `Broker.Send`'s invariant comment is unchanged — its rule "don't call with `toUID == sender` from inside an Update path" is exactly what the new code obeys.

437			})
438		}
439		m.sent = target.UserID
440		return m, nil
441	}

277	func newMailComposeModel(deps Deps, recipient string, parentID int64) mailComposeModel {
278		to := textinput.New()
279		to.Placeholder = "alice"
280		to.CharLimit = 12
281		to.Width = 30
282		to.SetValue(recipient)
283	
284		subj := textinput.New()
285		subj.Placeholder = "subject"
286		subj.CharLimit = 64
287		subj.Width = 60
288	
289		body := textarea.New()
290		body.Placeholder = "message body…"
291		body.CharLimit = 4000
292		body.SetWidth(60)
293		body.SetHeight(8)
294	
295		// On reply, default the subject from the parent (with "Re: " prefix
296		// once and only once).
297		if parentID != 0 && deps.Store != nil {
298			if parent, err := deps.Store.Mail().GetByID(context.Background(), parentID); err == nil {
299				s := parent.Subject
300				if !strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(s), "re:") {
301					s = "Re: " + s
302				}
303				subj.SetValue(s)
304			}
305		}
306	
307		m := mailComposeModel{
308			deps:     deps,
309			parentID: parentID,
310			to:       to,
311			subject:  subj,
312			body:     body,
313		}
314		switch {
315		case recipient == "":
316			m.focus = mailFocusTo
317			m.to.Focus()
318		case subj.Value() == "":
319			m.focus = mailFocusSubject
320			m.subject.Focus()
321		default:
322			m.focus = mailFocusBody
323			m.body.Focus()
324		}
325		return m
326	}

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Mail: self-mail memo + email-style reply quoting

Two fixes on screen_mail composer.

(1) Self-mail had the identical deadlock as self-WB before the previous
commit: Broker.Send to your own session blocks on the unbuffered
bubbletea msgs channel because nobody reads p.msgs while Update is
still executing. Same root cause documented in
pitfalls/water-balloon-self-send-hangs-server.md, just a different
surface — reported via a "寫信 New Mail" form with `to=david` (the
sender) and Ctrl+S hangs the session.

Fix mirrors the WB sidestep: when target.ID == from.ID, skip
Broker.Send entirely and call Mail().MarkRead(mail.ID) immediately so
the unread counter doesn't bump on reconnect for a memo the user just
wrote. Non-self path is unchanged.

(2) Reply now pre-fills the body with the parent in markdown
blockquote form so the thread reads like an email exchange:

  > alice · 2026-05-06 14:23 寫道:
  >
  > previous body line 1
  > previous body line 2

  <cursor>

Empty parent lines stay prefixed with `> ` so the blockquote remains
contiguous (RFC 3676 / standard email-client convention). Attribution
line names sender + timestamp from the parent — informative for
multi-participant threads. Cursor lands after the trailing blank line
so the user can start typing the reply without manual cursor moves.

Helper `quoteForReply(*store.Mail) string` lives next to submit; the
constructor calls it whenever parentID is non-zero, after the existing
"Re: " subject prefix logic.

Tests: TestMailCompose_AllowsSelfMemo (success path: no error, sent
flag set, row exists with read_at populated — the auto-mark-read pair
proves the broker path was skipped); TestMailCompose_ReplyQuotesParent
Body (attribution line + every parent line prefixed with `> ` +
trailing blank line). Existing TestMailCompose_ReplySetsParent still
passes — the SetValue on body in the test (`m.body.SetValue("ok")`)
overwrites the pre-fill, so the ParentID/ThreadID assertions are
unaffected.

Pitfall doc updated: the "future feature" note about mail's broker
trap is now a "fixed alongside" note pointing to the screen_mail.go
guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:44:14Z)**_

Shipped (**75f0f99**).

**Self-mail**: same skip-`Broker.Send`-when-target==self pattern as the wb fix, plus auto-`Mail().MarkRead` so a memo doesn't bump the unread counter on reconnect. Same root cause as the wb hang — bubbletea v1.x `msgs` is unbuffered, broker.Send-to-self deadlocks the Update loop. Pitfall doc updated to flag mail as "fixed alongside" rather than "future risk".

**Reply quoting**: when you press `r` on a mail thread, the compose body now pre-fills with:

```
> alice · 2026-05-06 14:23 寫道:
>
> previous body line 1
> previous body line 2

<cursor>
```

Markdown blockquote (`> ` prefix) on every line including blanks (RFC 3676 convention so the quote block stays contiguous), attribution line at the top with sender + timestamp from the parent, trailing blank line so the cursor sits ready below the quote. The existing `Re:` subject prefix is unchanged. Helper `quoteForReply(*store.Mail) string` lives next to `submit` for symmetry.

**Tests**: 2 new — `TestMailCompose_AllowsSelfMemo` (no error, row exists, `ReadAt` valid — that pair only happens together when broker was skipped); `TestMailCompose_ReplyQuotesParentBody` (attribution + every line prefixed + trailing blank). Existing `TestMailCompose_ReplySetsParent` keeps passing because it `SetValue`s the body explicitly, which overwrites the pre-fill.

Manual smoke: `ssh david@localhost -p 2222` → 信箱 → `c` → To `david` Subject `yo` Body `damn` Ctrl+S → no hang, returns to inbox, the row is there marked read; opening the thread and pressing `r` shows the original quoted with `>` and your cursor below.

---

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// glamourFallbackWidth is the wrap width used before we get a
16	// WindowSizeMsg. 80 is the historical terminal default; the renderer
17	// re-runs with the real width as soon as the first resize lands.
18	const glamourFallbackWidth = 80
19	
20	type articleViewModel struct {
21		deps    Deps
22		article *store.Article
23		pushes  []*store.Push
24		width   int
25		height  int
26		scroll  int
27		loadErr error
28	
29		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
30		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
31		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
32		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
33		rendered      string
34		renderedWidth int
35	
36		// push input state
37		pushing  bool
38		pushKind store.PushKind
39		pushIn   textinput.Model
40		err      string
41	
42		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
43		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
44		pendingDelete bool
45		pushCursor    int
46	}
47	
48	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
49	// to the current viewport. Falls back to the raw body on any glamour
50	// error so a corrupt-markdown article is still readable.
51	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
52		if m.article == nil {
53			m.rendered = ""
54			m.renderedWidth = 0
55			return
56		}
57		width := m.width - 4
58		if width < 40 {
59			width = glamourFallbackWidth
60		}
61		r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
62			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
63			glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
64		)
65		if err != nil {
66			m.rendered = m.article.Body
67			m.renderedWidth = width
68			return
69		}
70		out, err := r.Render(m.article.Body)
71		if err != nil {
72			m.rendered = m.article.Body
73			m.renderedWidth = width
74			return
75		}
76		// Trim leading/trailing blank lines glamour likes to add for
77		// breathing room — they push the content off-screen on small
78		// viewports. Internal blank lines stay.
79		m.rendered = strings.Trim(out, "\n")
80		m.renderedWidth = width
81	}
82	
83	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
84		ctx := context.Background()
85		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
86		if err != nil {
87			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
88		}
89		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
90	
91		ti := textinput.New()
92		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
93		ti.CharLimit = 80
94		ti.Width = 60
95	
96		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
97		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
98		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
99		m.renderBody()
100		return m
```

135	// =====================================================================
136	// Thread view
137	// =====================================================================
138	
139	type mailThreadModel struct {
140		deps     Deps
141		threadID int64
142		items    []*store.Mail
143		scroll   int
144		width    int
145		height   int
146		loadErr  error
147	}
148	
149	func newMailThreadModel(deps Deps, threadID int64) mailThreadModel {
150		ctx := context.Background()
151		items, err := deps.Store.Mail().ListThread(ctx, threadID)
152		if err == nil {
153			// Mark every mail in the thread that's addressed to the current user
154			// as read. A thread you've opened is no longer "new".
155			for _, it := range items {
156				if it.ToUserID == deps.User.ID && !it.ReadAt.Valid {
157					_ = deps.Store.Mail().MarkRead(ctx, it.ID)
158				}
159			}
160		}
161		return mailThreadModel{deps: deps, threadID: threadID, items: items, loadErr: err}
162	}
163	
164	func (m mailThreadModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
165	
166	func (m mailThreadModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
167		switch msg := msg.(type) {
168		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
169			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
170			return m, nil
171		case tea.KeyMsg:
172			switch msg.String() {
173			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
174				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMailInbox} }
175			case "Q":
176				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
177			case "up", "k":
178				if m.scroll > 0 {
179					m.scroll--
180				}
181			case "down", "j":
182				m.scroll++
183			case "g":
184				m.scroll = 0
185			case "r":
186				// Reply to the LAST message in the thread (whoever sent it).
187				if len(m.items) == 0 {
188					return m, nil
189				}
190				last := m.items[len(m.items)-1]
191				recipient := last.FromUserIDStr
192				if last.FromUserID == m.deps.User.ID && last.ParentID.Valid {
193					// We sent the last one; reply to the user we were replying to.
194					if parent, err := m.deps.Store.Mail().GetByID(context.Background(), last.ParentID.Int64); err == nil {
195						recipient = parent.FromUserIDStr
196					}
197				}
198				return m, func() tea.Msg {
199					return NavigateMsg{
200						To:        ScreenMailCompose,
201						Recipient: recipient,
202						MailID:    last.ID,
203					}
204				}
205			}
206		}
207		return m, nil
208	}
209	
210	func (m mailThreadModel) View() string {
211		var b strings.Builder
212		b.WriteString("\n")
213		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 信件 Thread #%d ", m.threadID)))
214		b.WriteString("\n\n")
215	
216		if m.loadErr != nil {
217			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
218			return b.String()
219		}
220		if len(m.items) == 0 {
221			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("(empty thread)") + "\n")
222			return b.String()
223		}
224	
225		for i, it := range m.items {
226			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── #%d  %s → %s  %s ──",
227				i+1,
228				it.FromUserIDStr,
229				recipientName(m.deps, it.ToUserID),
230				it.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
231			)) + "\n")
232			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Subject: ") + it.Subject + "\n")
233			b.WriteString("  " + indent(it.Body, "  ") + "\n\n")
234		}
235	
236		b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("r reply · Esc/←/h back · Q quit"))
237		b.WriteString("\n")
238		return b.String()
239	}
240	
241	// recipientName looks up the display handle for a user_id; falls back to
242	// the numeric id if the lookup fails (best-effort, View() must not error).
243	func recipientName(deps Deps, userID int64) string {
244		if deps.Store == nil {
245			return fmt.Sprintf("uid=%d", userID)
246		}
247		u, err := deps.Store.Users().GetByID(context.Background(), userID)
248		if err != nil || u == nil {
249			return fmt.Sprintf("uid=%d", userID)

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
package tui

import (
	"strings"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
)

// glamourFallbackWidth is the wrap width used before a tea.WindowSizeMsg
// has set the real terminal width. 80 is the historical terminal default;
// any screen that caches a glamour render should re-render once the
// actual width arrives.
const glamourFallbackWidth = 80

// renderMarkdown runs body through glamour with word-wrap set to width.
// Returns the raw body unchanged on any glamour failure so a corrupt-
// markdown payload is still readable. Trims the leading/trailing blank
// lines glamour likes to add for breathing room — they push content off
// small viewports.
func renderMarkdown(body string, width int) string {
	if width < 40 {
		width = glamourFallbackWidth
	}
	r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
		glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
		glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
	)
	if err != nil {
		return body
	}
	out, err := r.Render(body)
	if err != nil {
		return body
	}
	return strings.Trim(out, "\n")
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit

```bash
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Mail thread: glamour-render bodies (parity with article view)

Mail bodies are now rendered through glamour with word-wrap set to the
viewport, matching the in-terminal markdown formatting articles already
get (commit f3c1d5d). Headings, code blocks, lists, and the new reply
quote-blocks (`> `-prefixed) all render with the same dark-style theme
as articles, so a user reading a mail thread sees the same visual
treatment they'd see reading an article.

Refactor: extract the glamour invocation into a reusable
`renderMarkdown(body, width) string` helper in `markdown_render.go`,
along with the `glamourFallbackWidth` constant. articleViewModel.render
Body now delegates to it; mailThreadModel gains a parallel renderBodies
method that renders all items in the thread at once.

Caching: rendered bodies live on mailThreadModel as `[]string`
(parallel to items[]), invalidated on WindowSizeMsg width change.
Same rationale as article view — glamour's first call loads chroma's
syntax highlighter (~tens of ms) and View runs on every keystroke;
re-rendering N message bodies per keypress would lag visibly.

renderBodies is called once at construction (using
glamourFallbackWidth=80) so the thread is readable even before the
first WindowSizeMsg lands; Update re-renders on resize. Fallback to
the raw `it.Body` if rendering hasn't run yet (defense-in-depth — the
constructor would have to silently fail for this to matter).

Tests: TestMailThread_GlamourRendersMarkdown (ANSI escapes present,
heading + body text preserved through stripANSI),
TestMailThread_GlamourReRendersOnResize (renderedWidth tracks the
current viewport).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-06 07:51:29Z)**_

Shipped (**5b99534**).

**What changed**: mail thread bodies now go through the same `glamour` pipeline articles use — same dark style, same word-wrap, same `# heading` / `code` / list rendering. The reply blockquotes you just got (`> alice 寫道:`) render as proper visual blockquotes too.

**Refactor**: extracted `renderMarkdown(body, width) string` into `internal/tui/markdown_render.go` along with the `glamourFallbackWidth` constant. `articleViewModel.renderBody` now delegates to it; `mailThreadModel` gets a parallel `renderBodies` that renders all items at once. No new dependency — glamour was already in the module.

**Caching**: `mailThreadModel.rendered []string` parallel to `items[]`, invalidated on `WindowSizeMsg` when width changes. Without the cache, every keystroke would re-render N message bodies (glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter — visibly laggy on a long thread).

**Tests**: 2 new — ANSI escapes present + heading text preserved (mirrors article view's), and width-change re-render. All `go test -race ./...` green.

Manual smoke: write a mail with `# Heading`, lists, a code fence — open the thread and you'll see the same lipgloss-styled rendering articles get. Reply with `r` and the parent body shows up as a proper blockquote, then your typed reply renders on top in the same thread view.
